fronts.title: Lebanon / Hezbollah
fronts.status: ESCALATING DESPITE CEASEFIRE — OPERATION ETERNAL DARKNESS
fronts.summary:
Operation Eternal Darkness (Apr 8): Israel's largest single strike wave on Lebanon — ~50 IAF jets, ~160 munitions, 100+ Hezbollah command centers/military installations. Strikes hit Beirut southern suburbs, central areas, Beqaa Valley, southern Lebanon. 254 killed, 1,000+ injured. Five IDF divisions in Lebanon (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd). 98th Division near full control of Bint Jbail (Apr 9). 7 key bridges destroyed; southern Lebanon cut off. Hezbollah resumed rocket attacks on northern Israel (dozens fired Apr 9).

Netanyahu: "Lebanon is NOT in the ceasefire." Vance: "We never made such a commitment." Arab League: Israel "persistently seeking to sabotage" Iran ceasefire. IDF announced area south of Litani River "disconnected from Lebanon." Lebanon declared day of mourning. 1,784+ killed total in Lebanon; 1M+ displaced.

fronts.title: Strait of Hormuz
fronts.summary:
Ceasefire Day 1 (Apr 8): Only 4 vessels passed with AIS active (Kpler data). Iran re-blocked after Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness. By Apr 9: oil tankers halted again per Iran's Fars News. IRGC published mine chart routing ships northward near Larak Island — implies mines in traditional Oman-side corridor. Cap: ≤15 vessels/day with IRGC approval; fees: ~$1/barrel (Iran's share for reconstruction). 400+ tankers stuck in Gulf. UK FM: Hormuz "must be toll-free."

Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

fronts.title: Houthi / Red Sea
fronts.summary:
Apr 6: Joint Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah cruise missile + UAV operation at Eilat. 8+ Houthi attacks on Israel total. All Axis of Resistance groups announced 2-week suspension of attacks on "enemy bases" following ceasefire — but Hezbollah resumed after Lebanon strikes. Houthis have NOT resumed commercial shipping attacks.

Senior Iranian adviser Velayati (Apr 5): Bab al-Mandab can be closed "with a single signal." Houthi-Iran joint operation targeting Eilat confirms deep coordination. Bab al-Mandab remains open but explicitly threatened as escalation option.

fronts.title: Iran Air Campaign
fronts.summary:
13,000+ strikes over 38 days of major combat (CENTCOM Admiral Cooper, Apr 9). Iranian air force grounded; 150+ naval vessels destroyed; 190+ ballistic missile launchers destroyed; ~80% of nuclear industrial base struck. Ceasefire halted strikes as of Apr 7. Threatened to resume if ceasefire collapses.

CENTCOM: 90% Iran weapons factories hit, 80% missile facilities, 80% nuclear industrial base. VP Vance: energy infrastructure strikes — next threshold if talks fail. Politico: Pentagon officials warned US was "running out of strategically important targets."

fronts.title: Iraqi Militias
fronts.summary:
Pro-Iranian groups in Iraq announced parallel 2-week suspension of attacks on "enemy bases" following ceasefire. Previously: 19–41 drone/rocket attacks/day by Islamic Resistance in Iraq; BP North Rumaila oilfield hit (Apr 4); Chinese-operated Maysan oilfield hit (Apr 5). Ceasefire compliance uncertain given Hezbollah resumed after Lebanon strikes.

Apr 5: Chinese-operated Maysan oilfield struck — first targeting of Chinese-operated infrastructure. US counter-strikes hit 53rd PMF Brigade HQ (Tal Afar, Apr 1) and 45th PMF Brigade HQ (Anbar, Apr 4). Militia pause may dissolve if Lebanon ceasefire collapses.

The U.S. said last week that it would seize sanctioned ships.

events.title: UNSC: Russia and China Veto Bahrain's Hormuz Resolution
events.detail: The UN Security Council voted on a sixth-revised Bahrain-drafted resolution on free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Result: 11 in favor, 2 vetoes (Russia and China), 2 abstentions. Russia's Ambassador Nebenzya and China's Ambassador Fu Cong blamed the war "squarely on the US and Israel" and said the priority was halting military operations — not acting against Iran. The original draft had invoked Chapter VII authorization for force; six revisions stripped all coercive language. Even this thoroughly watered-down text was vetoed, leaving the UNSC paralyzed on Hormuz enforcement.

events.title: Dated Brent Physical Oil Hits Record $144.42/Barrel
events.detail: Dated Brent (the physical crude benchmark published by Platts since 1987) hit an all-time record of $144.42/barrel on April 7 — surpassing all prior records including the 2008 peak of $147.50 for WTI. Brent futures traded near $109 while WTI (CLK26 May futures) reached ~$116 (+3.67% on the day). March 2026 had already logged the largest single-month crude oil price gain in recorded history. Analysts at Macquarie described crude as behaving "like a meme stock until peace is achieved."

events.title: Kharg Island Struck (Second Time) — Military Targets Only, Oil Spared
events.detail: US forces struck military targets on Kharg Island overnight April 6-7 for the second time in the war — hitting approximately 50 locations including bunkers, a military installation, and ammunition depots. VP Vance explicitly stated the calibration: "We will not strike energy and infrastructure targets until the Iranians either present a proposal we can support or fail to put forth one." Iranian state media confirmed oil infrastructure remained intact. Kharg Island processes approximately 90% of Iran's oil exports. In retaliation, Iran's IRGC struck the Jubail petrochemical facility in Saudi Arabia.

events.title: IRGC Jubail Retaliation Strike — Saudi Petrochemical Complex Hit
events.detail: Following the US strike on Kharg Island's military targets, Iran's IRGC struck the Jubail petrochemical facility in Saudi Arabia in direct retaliation. Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman had previously vowed to use force against any Iranian incursions. Saudi Arabia had closed its airspace to US and Israeli aircraft while publicly condemning Iranian strikes — a contradictory position that reflected Riyadh's attempt to avoid deeper involvement in the conflict.

events.title: Trump: "A Whole Civilization Will Die Tonight" — Infrastructure Strike Threat
events.detail: President Trump posted on Truth Social on April 7: "A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again. I don't want that to happen, but it probably will." Trump threatened "complete demolition" of Iran's power plants and bridges, and bombing Iran "back to the Stone Ages." Former ICC Prosecutor Luis Moreno Ocampo stated the threats "could constitute war crimes under the same legal framework used to indict Russian officials over Ukraine." Amnesty International called for urgent global action to prevent "atrocity crimes." Trump told reporters he was "not at all" worried about war crime accusations.
events.sourceText: ABC News / US News / Amnesty International / Harvard Crimson (April 7, 2026)

events.title: Pakistan PM Sharif Requests 2-Week Deadline Extension
events.detail: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif publicly posted on X asking Trump to extend the April 7 8 PM ET deadline by two weeks, and asked Iran to reopen the Strait as a goodwill gesture: "Diplomatic efforts for peaceful settlement of the ongoing war in the Middle East are progressing steadily, strongly and powerfully with the potential to lead to substantive results in near future." White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Trump "has been made aware of the proposal." Trump told Fox News Sharif was "a highly respected man."

events.title: Iran Ambassador: Pakistan Talks at "Critical, Sensitive Stage"
events.detail: Iran's Ambassador to Pakistan Reza Amiri Moghadam posted on X: "Positive and productive endeavours in Good Will and Good Office to stop the war is approaching a critical, sensitive stage. Stay tuned." The statement indicated Iran was aware of the diplomatic momentum while stopping short of signaling concession on any core demand. Pakistan remained the sole communication channel between Washington and Tehran, with the MOU to be finalized electronically through Islamabad.

events.title: 10 Senior IRGC Commanders Killed — Days 32-39 Targeted Killing Tally
events.detail: Between March 31 and April 7, US-Israeli targeted killings eliminated at least 10 senior IRGC and Artesh commanders: Eshaghi (AFGS adviser), Mohammad Sadeghi (IRGC missile tunnel engineer), Mehdi Vafaei (Quds Force Lebanon underground chief), Makram Atimi (ballistic missile chief), Gen. Mohammad Ali Fathali Zadeh (Fatehin Battalions), Yusuf Ismail Hashem (Hezbollah Southern Front commander), and multiple battalion-level officers. Adding to the 28 named senior figures eliminated in Days 1-31, Iran had lost approximately 38+ general and senior officers killed in six weeks of war — an unprecedented command attrition rate.
events.sourceText: ISW / Critical Threats / Wikipedia (2026 Iran War) (Days 32-39)

events.title: Hormuz: 53 Weekly Transits — War-Era High But 93% Below Normal
events.detail: Bloomberg's Hormuz tracker recorded 53 vessel transits in the past week — the highest since the war began, but still 93% below the pre-war norm of 840+ weekly transits. A small trickle of "approved" traffic moved via an Iranian-authorized corridor skirting Iranian territorial waters near Qeshm and Larak islands. Iran declared Iraq, China, India, Russia, and Pakistan face no restrictions. The informal $2M per-ship toll was already being collected; at least one confirmed payment was reported by Lloyd's List. Iran's Parliament was reviewing legislation to formalize the toll regime.
events.sourceText: Bloomberg / Al Jazeera / Lloyd's List / NYT (April 4-7, 2026)

events.title: War Powers Resolution Fails in Senate 47-53 — No Congressional AUMF
events.detail: Sen. Tim Kaine (D-VA) and Sen. Rand Paul (R-KY)'s War Powers Resolution requiring Trump to remove US forces from Iran hostilities absent congressional authorization failed in the Senate 47-53, voting largely along party lines (Paul voted yes; Sen. Fetterman (D-PA) voted no, posting "My vote IS Operation Epic Fury"). A parallel House resolution by Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) also fell short. No Authorization for Use of Military Force (AUMF) has been passed. The war is being conducted solely on Article II Commander-in-Chief authority, without congressional declaration.

events.title: Gold Hits $4,676/oz — S&P 500 Down 9.3% Since War Began
events.detail: Gold spot price reached $4,676.04/oz on April 7 (+0.42% on the day), up 18% from $3,964/oz on February 27. The S&P 500 was down approximately 9.3% since the war began. Goldman Sachs year-end gold target: $5,400/oz; J.P. Morgan: $6,300/oz. Bitcoin failed as a safe haven, declining from its $126,000 ATH (October 2025) to ~$67,000 — a 47% drop — while gold surged. The University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index reached 53.3 in March 2026 — lowest since December 2025, with year-ahead inflation expectations jumping from 3.4% to 3.8%.
events.sourceText: Yahoo Finance / Natural Resource Stocks / Investopedia (April 7, 2026)

events.title: Scahill: Iran's Actual Ceasefire Terms Obtained — Includes War Reparations and Hormuz Sovereignty
events.detail: Drop Site News (Scahill) obtained Iran's actual counter-proposal submitted via Pakistan intermediary. Iran's terms: (1) permanent end to war, not temporary ceasefire; (2) ceasefire covering Lebanon, Iraq, and Palestine as well as Iran; (3) war reparations paid to Iran; (4) full sanctions relief; (5) Iran retaining sovereignty over Hormuz; (6) nuclear enrichment terms (details withheld). Iran's position: "The Americans are not prepared to engage in what can genuinely be called negotiations. Rather, they seek to impose their own terms."

events.title: ICC Prosecutor Ocampo: Trump Threats Could Constitute War Crimes
events.detail: Former ICC Prosecutor Luis Moreno Ocampo stated at Harvard that Trump's April 7 threats to destroy all Iranian power plants and bridges "could constitute war crimes under the same legal framework used to indict Russian officials over Ukraine." DAWN advocacy group called on Iran to file an Article 12(3) ICC declaration granting the Court jurisdiction over crimes on Iranian territory from February 28. Over 100 international law experts had signed a Just Security open letter documenting violations by both parties including UN Charter breach, targeting of leaders, oil/water infrastructure, and "denial of quarter" threats by Defense Secretary Hegseth on March 13.
events.sourceText: The Harvard Crimson / DAWN / Time Magazine / Bloomberg (April 7, 2026)

events.title: Oxford Economics: Prolonged War Could Tip World Into Recession
events.detail: Oxford Economics projected that if the Hormuz closure persists 6 months: global inflation would reach 7.7% (near 2022 peak); Brent crude would surge to ~$190/barrel by August (above the 2008 all-time record); Asian/European gas would reach $30/MMBtu in Q3; global GDP would grow only 1.4% in 2026 (1.2 percentage points below baseline); and the world economy would contract mid-year in the first synchronized contraction outside a pandemic or global financial crisis in 40 years. Goldman Sachs: 30% US recession probability; EY-Parthenon: 40% probability of severe US downturn.
events.sourceText: Oxford Economics / Goldman Sachs / CBS News (April 2026)

events.title: Ali Al Salem Air Base Hit — 15 Americans Injured in Shahed-136 Strike
events.detail: Iranian Shahed-136 loitering munitions struck Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait overnight April 5-6. 15 US military personnel were injured in the attack — the most significant US personnel casualties at a Gulf base in days. The base had already been heavily targeted in previous strikes. DoD confirmed the injuries. Kuwait's government publicly condemned the strike while privately requesting the US reduce its military footprint.

events.title: Trump Threatens Journalists Over Second Downed Airman Report
events.detail: President Trump threatened to jail journalists who reported that a second US Air Force officer was missing after being shot down. Trump called such reports "treason" and threatened media companies with prosecution. The CPJ had documented 7 journalists killed and 16 targeted, assaulted, or harassed as of Day 39. FCC Chair Brendan Carr had previously (March 14) threatened to revoke broadcasters' licenses over Iran war coverage.

events.title: Iran Rejects US 45-Day Ceasefire Proposal as "Illogical"
events.detail: Iranian FM spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei publicly called the US-brokered 45-day ceasefire proposal "illogical" and "unacceptable," rejecting the framework delivered via Pakistan. Iran's stated reasons: it cannot negotiate under military threat while US strikes continue; temporary ceasefires benefit the US by giving time to rearm; the 15-point US plan was "exceedingly ambitious." Iran submitted its own 10-point counter-proposal including full war reparations, permanent cessation of all regional conflicts, full US military withdrawal from Gulf states, and Iran's right to charge Hormuz transit fees.

events.title: Iran's 10-Point Counter-Proposal: War Reparations, Hormuz Fees, Lebanon Included
events.detail: Iran's formal counter-proposal (submitted via Pakistan, April 6-7) demanded: end to all regional conflicts including Lebanon; full withdrawal of US military from Gulf states; permanent ceasefire with guarantees Iran will never be attacked again; full war reparations and reconstruction compensation; Iran's legal jurisdiction over Hormuz with right to charge fees by ship type and cargo; lifting of all sanctions; and rejection of any temporary ceasefire. Iran refused to accept any deadline while reviewing proposals. Trump described Iran's counter as "not good enough, but a very significant step."
events.sourceText: Chosun Ilbo / Al Jazeera / Arab News Japan (April 7, 2026)

events.title: Gulf Sovereign Wealth Funds Review US Tech and Media Investments
events.detail: Drop Site News (Scahill) reported that Gulf sovereign wealth funds were conducting a sweeping review of US investments, including funding for US artificial intelligence data centers, the Paramount/Skydance merger, and the Warner Bros./Discovery merger. The review reflected Gulf state anger at the war's economic devastation and signaled a potential financial rupture between Gulf capital and US markets. Qatar's FM al-Thani spoke with Iranian FM Araghchi on April 7, urging Iran to stop allowing Qatar's US bases to be used.

events.title: Houthis Claim Joint Cruise Missile and UAV Attack on Israel With Iran and Hezbollah
events.detail: IDF reported several UAVs from Yemen aimed at Eilat in southern Israel. Houthis claimed a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah combining cruise missiles and UAVs. All were intercepted. The coordinated three-front announcement — Houthis, Hezbollah, and Iran simultaneously — was intended to demonstrate the persistence of the "Axis of Resistance's" operational coordination despite six weeks of sustained coalition strikes.
events.sourceText: IDF / Wikipedia (2026 Houthi Strikes on Israel) (April 6, 2026)

events.title: CPJ: 7 Journalists Killed, 11 Detained in 38 Days of War
events.detail: The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) documented the following as of April 6: 7 journalists killed; 1 journalist kidnapped (US freelancer Shelly Kittleson abducted in Baghdad); 16 journalists targeted, assaulted, or harassed; 9 media outlets damaged in airstrikes; 11 journalists detained; 19 journalists obstructed. Qatar arrested 300+ people for sharing images of Iranian attacks. Bahrain arrested 4 people for "treason" for filming Iranian strikes. Iran's judiciary ordered asset freezes on 100+ journalists and 63 Iran International managers.

events.title: IFRC Alert: CHF 40M Emergency Appeal Only 6% Funded — Overland Turkey Route Attempted
events.detail: The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies reported its CHF 40 million emergency appeal for Iran operations was only 6% funded. IFRC Head of Delegation Maria Martinez: "Needs are exponentially increasing. Resources are not unlimited." Three IFRC workers had been killed on duty, including one in a March 31 airstrike on a medical clinic in Zanjan Province. With Iran's Hormuz blockade preventing supply shipments from Dubai, the IFRC was attempting to ship 1 million Swiss francs' worth of trauma kits overland from Turkey — a journey taking weeks. 1,900+ people killed and 21,000+ injured in Iran since February 28.

events.title: Hezbollah Anti-Ship Cruise Missile Strikes Israeli Warship
events.detail: Hezbollah fired an anti-ship cruise missile at an Israeli Navy warship operating in Lebanese coastal waters — the first confirmed Hezbollah anti-ship cruise missile strike of the conflict. The missile hit and caused confirmed damage to the vessel. The attack demonstrated Hezbollah's retention of advanced anti-ship capabilities despite Israel's claim to have significantly degraded Hezbollah. Hezbollah's Telegram channel posted footage of the launch.

events.title: Missile Launcher Sites Struck in Mahabad and Kashan
events.detail: US-Israeli forces struck a Qasam missile launcher (1,400 km range) in Mahabad, Kurdistan Province, and a missile launcher in a warehouse in Kashan, Isfahan Province. Ahvaz International Airport in Khuzestan Province was also struck; the Haz-e Hoat Independent Armored Brigade operating nearby sustained losses. The IRGC Quds Force 1st Imam Hussein Naval District HQ in Isfahan Province was hit in a combined force strike.

events.title: Houthis Conduct 8th Attack on Israel — Coordination With Iran and Hezbollah
events.detail: Houthis launched their 8th attack on Israel since March 28, targeting Ben Gurion Airport and military sites in southern Israel with a cluster missile and several drones. All were intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Houthis claimed coordination with Iran and Hezbollah in a joint operation. The Houthis had not yet resumed commercial shipping attacks in the Red Sea as of April 7, but explicitly threatened to impose a naval blockade at Bab al-Mandab targeting "aggressor country" vessels.

events.title: Trump Issues Expletive-Laden Truth Social Ultimatum — April 7 Deadline Set
events.detail: President Trump posted an expletive-filled Truth Social ultimatum confirming a final deadline of April 7 at 8 PM ET for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatened to strike every Iranian power plant and bridge if the deadline was not met. This was his third deadline extension since the initial March 21 ultimatum. White House simultaneously denied any intention to use nuclear weapons.
events.sourceText: Fox News / PBS NewsHour / ABC News

events.title: Drop Site News Scoop: Witkoff Texting Araghchi, Iran Ignoring Messages
events.detail: Drop Site News (Jeremy Scahill) reported that Trump's Special Envoy Steve Witkoff had been personally texting Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi requesting talks. Iran was ignoring the messages. Two senior Iranian officials confirmed the story to Drop Site. This directly contradicted the White House narrative that Iran was "begging" for negotiations. Within an hour of publication, a US official offered Axios a counter-spin claiming Araghchi had reached out. The White House called Drop Site a "radical, left-wing outlet carrying water for the Iranian terrorist regime."

events.title: IEA 400 Million Barrel SPR Release — Largest in History
events.detail: The International Energy Agency's 400 million barrel coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve release — announced March 11 and now underway — was assessed by analysts as insufficient. The release covers only approximately 26 days of Hormuz-disrupted lost supply (~15M bbl/day). Since the announcement, Brent had surged an additional 17%+ to new highs. Bernstein: "It will have a minimal effect on the trajectory of oil prices." IEA Director Birol: "The key to restoring stable oil and gas flows lies in reopening the Strait of Hormuz." The US contribution is 172 million barrels (43% of total) from the SPR over ~120 days.
events.sourceText: NYT / Axios / CNBC / Al Jazeera (March 11 announcement)

events.title: Philippines Declares State of Emergency Over Oil Crisis
events.detail: The Philippines declared a state of emergency over the oil supply crisis triggered by the Hormuz blockade. The Philippine peso had already fallen to a record low of 60.1 PHP/USD on March 19. The state of emergency enabled emergency energy rationing measures and fast-tracked LNG import diversification contracts. Indonesia, Thailand, and Vietnam issued separate emergency energy advisories. The Hormuz blockade was disproportionately affecting Southeast Asian economies dependent on Gulf crude imports.
events.sourceText: Wikipedia (Economic Impact 2026 Iran War) / CSIS Southeast Asia analysis

events.title: Gen. Munir All-Night Mediation: Calls with Vance, Witkoff, and Araghchi
events.detail: Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir spent the night of April 5-6 "in contact all night long" with US Vice President JD Vance, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, and Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi. Pakistan delivered the US-led ceasefire framework proposal to Iran. The "Islamabad Accord" framework proposed a two-phase deal: immediate ceasefire and Hormuz reopening followed by 15-20 days for a comprehensive permanent settlement to be finalized electronically through Pakistan. VP Vance's two planned Islamabad visits had both been cancelled by Tehran at the last minute.

events.title: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Vicinity Struck — Security Guard Killed
events.detail: A projectile fragment struck near the auxiliary building of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, killing one physical protection staff member. Iran stated this was the fourth attack near the plant. IAEA confirmed no damage to the reactor and no increase in radiation levels. Russia's Rosatom evacuated 198 personnel from Bushehr on April 4, in addition to 163 previously evacuated on March 25. The near-miss of an operational nuclear reactor triggered emergency IAEA monitoring protocols.

events.title: Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone Struck — 5 Killed, 170 Injured
events.detail: US-Israeli forces struck the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone in Khuzestan Province, killing at least 5 and injuring 170. Iran's vice president described the alleged use of a bunker-buster bomb as a "symbol of Trump's madness." Three additional petrochemical facilities at Bandar-e Imam Khomeni were also struck: Fajr, Rejal, and Amir Kabir Petrochemical Companies. Iran's Sharif University of Technology was also hit; at least 34 people including 6 children were killed in April 4 strikes across Iran.

events.title: F-15E Rescue Operation Concludes — WSO Rescued After 36 Hours
events.detail: After 36 hours evading capture deep inside Iran, the missing Weapon Systems Officer (colonel) was located using CIA-provided special technology. Elite US Special Operations Forces, supported by 155 aircraft, conducted the extraction. Two MC-130 special operations transport aircraft were deliberately destroyed by US forces at an abandoned airstrip in Iran when they became stuck in sand and could not be extracted. Several MH-6 Little Bird helicopters were also deliberately destroyed. The WSO was rescued alive on April 5 with only a sprained ankle. Three IRGC members were killed during the rescue operation. No US service members were killed.
events.sourceText: Wikipedia (2026 F-15E Rescue Operation in Iran) / ISW (April 5, 2026)
sourceRefs: Wikipedia (2026 F-15E Rescue Operation in Iran)

events.title: Iranian Missile with Cluster Warhead Strikes Near IDF HaKirya HQ — Tel Aviv
events.detail: An Iranian ballistic missile with a cluster munition warhead struck in the vicinity of the IDF HaKirya (Kirya) headquarters compound in central Tel Aviv. Shrapnel fell across multiple central Israel sites, wounding at least 6 people. The strike demonstrated Iran's continued ability to target Israel's central command infrastructure despite over 5 weeks of coalition strikes on its missile infrastructure.

events.title: BP North Rumaila Oilfield Hit — Iraqi Workers Wounded
events.detail: Iraq-based Iranian-aligned militia drones struck the BP-operated North Rumaila oilfield in Basra Province, wounding 3 Iraqi workers. It was the first confirmed targeting of British-operated energy infrastructure in Iraq by pro-Iran militias. The Kataib Hezbollah-controlled 45th PMF Brigade HQ in Anbar Province was struck in retaliation by US-Israeli forces, killing 2 PMF fighters. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed 19 drone and rocket attacks against US bases the same day.

events.title: IDF 91st Division: 1,000+ Hezbollah Operatives Killed Since March 2
events.detail: The IDF announced its 91st Division had killed over 1,000 Hezbollah operatives since March 2, including hundreds of Radwan Force members. The 282nd Artillery Brigade fired over 400 shells at Hezbollah targets in a single 24-hour period on April 4. IDF conducted 140 airstrikes on Hezbollah targets over April 3-4. Hezbollah conducted 19 attacks against IDF in southern Lebanon and 23 attacks on northern Israeli communities including rocket barrages at Kiryat Shmona.

events.title: Mearsheimer: Americans Want Regime Change, Israelis Want Failed State
events.detail: Prof. John Mearsheimer appeared on Judging Freedom on April 4, articulating the divergent US and Israeli war objectives: "The Americans want regime change. The Israelis want to create a failed state. They want to fragment Iran the same way they fragmented and destroyed Iraq." He warned Iran has "no incentive to quit" given the military balance and the stakes involved. Col. Wilkerson (appearing with Chris Hedges on ScheerPost the same day) explicitly compared a potential Iran ground invasion to Vietnam and warned of serious risk of Israeli nuclear escalation if conventional operations fail.

events.title: Iran Food Inflation: +105% Year-on-Year — Poultry Farms Shut Down
events.detail: Iranian internal economic data showed year-on-year food inflation of 105% by early April 2026: oils/fats +219%, bread/cereals +140%, meat/poultry +135%, dairy +117%, fruits +104%. Poultry farms shut down due to fuel and electricity shortages, causing a 90% drop in domestic meat production. Humanitarian agencies warned of acute malnutrition risk. The Iranian Rial was trading at 1,616,000 IRR/USD on the black market — effectively 38 times the official rate.

events.title: F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down Over Iran — First US Combat Aircraft Loss
events.detail: US Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle (call sign "Dude 44"), from the 494th Fighter Squadron at RAF Lakenheath, UK, was shot down over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province in southwestern Iran — the first confirmed US combat aircraft loss to Iranian fire in the war. Both crew ejected: the pilot was recovered by CSAR forces the same day. The Weapon Systems Officer (WSO, rank of colonel) remained missing; Iran announced a bounty for his capture. A recovery helicopter came under small arms fire and returned safely to Iraq.
events.sourceText: Iran International / Wikipedia (2026 F-15E Rescue Operation in Iran) / BBC
sourceRefs: Wikipedia (2026 F-15E Rescue Operation in Iran)

events.title: A-10 Thunderbolt II Downed Near Strait of Hormuz
events.detail: A US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II was downed near the Strait of Hormuz while providing CSAR support for the F-15E rescue operation. Iran released footage claiming a surface-to-air missile shootdown. The DoD confirmed the A-10 was hit by Iranian fire; the pilot ejected and was recovered. Larry Johnson (former CIA analyst) later claimed the full CSAR rescue package included losses of 2 Black Hawk helicopters, 2 C-130 transport aircraft, and 4 MH-6 Little Birds — an estimated $400 million in aircraft destroyed in a single rescue operation.
events.sourceText: CBS News / Wikipedia (F-15E Rescue) / Larry Johnson (Judging Freedom, April 5)

events.title: IDF Halts Iran Strikes to Avoid Interfering With US CSAR
events.detail: The IDF halted all planned strike sorties against Iran on April 3 to avoid inadvertently interfering with the US Combat Search and Rescue (CSAR) operation for the downed F-15E crew. An unnamed senior Israeli official confirmed the stand-down to ISW. The IRGC claimed additional US aircraft were downed during the rescue mission, though Iran later revised these claims.

events.title: Scott Ritter Claims F-35 Shot Down — Iranian Air Defenses Active
events.detail: Scott Ritter (former UN weapons inspector) stated on Judging Freedom: "We don't have control of the skies... We're out of cruise missiles. We're out of joint standoff weapons. Now we have to go in. And as we go in, we're finding out that the Iranian air defense is very much active. They're shooting down our drones. They hit an F-35." Ritter's F-35 claim was not confirmed by DoD. He also cited five THAAD-associated radar destructions: "There's photographic evidence that five radars associated with THAAD batteries have been destroyed by the Iranians."
events.sourceText: Scott Ritter / Judging Freedom (April 5, 2026) — claimed, unconfirmed by DoD
sourceRefs: Judging Freedom (April 5, 2026) — claimed, unconfirmed by DoD

events.title: Larry Johnson: 40% of US THAAD Inventory Destroyed Over 6 Days
events.detail: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson stated on Judging Freedom: "Over the last six days, Iran has destroyed 40% of the total number of THAADs in the US inventory. We don't have that many. And when they're manufactured, you make maybe one or two maximum a year." Johnson confirmed Iranian strikes destroyed THAAD-associated AN/TPY and AN/FPS radar systems worth $500M-$1B each at Al-Salti Air Base (Jordan), Al Udeid Air Base (Qatar), and Fifth Fleet HQ (Bahrain). "We are now blinded. Before, they could get a 15-30 minute warning. Now we've got incoming missiles with maybe a minute of warning."
events.sourceText: Larry Johnson / Judging Freedom (April 3, 2026) — claimed, unconfirmed by DoD
sourceRefs: Judging Freedom (April 3, 2026) — claimed, unconfirmed by DoD

events.title: Intel Roundtable: Johnson and McGovern Debunk Trump's April 1 Speech
events.detail: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson and former CIA officer Ray McGovern appeared on Judging Freedom's Intel Roundtable to systematically rebut Trump's April 1 speech. They identified specific fabrications: Trump falsely attributed the 2000 USS Cole bombing to Iran (it was al-Qaeda); Trump falsely claimed Iran was "right at the doorstep" of nuclear weapons — directly contradicted by US IC assessments; Trump's claim of direct US-Iran talks was fabricated: "He has confabulated the entire notion that America and Iran are talking. No, they're not."
events.sourceText: Larry Johnson / Ray McGovern / Judging Freedom (April 3, 2026)

events.title: IRIB State Broadcast Transmitter Destroyed — Tehran
events.detail: US-Israeli combined forces struck the IRIB (Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting) strategic radio and TV transmitter in Jamaran, northern Tehran Province. Iranian media described it as the most strategically important broadcast transmitter in Iran. The strike was part of a broader information warfare campaign; Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran was also struck the same day, bringing the total universities hit to over 30.

events.title: UNIFIL Peacekeepers Injured — Hezbollah Projectile Hits UN Position
events.detail: A projectile struck a UNIFIL (UN Interim Force in Lebanon) position in Odaisseh, Marjaayoun District, injuring three UN peacekeepers. The IDF assessed Hezbollah as responsible. In a separate incident, one soldier from the Maglan Unit (89th Commando Brigade, 98th Paratrooper Division) was killed and another seriously wounded in an IDF friendly-fire incident in Shebaa, Hasbaya District.

events.title: B1 Bileghan Bridge Collapse — 8 Killed, 95 Wounded
events.detail: US forces struck the B1 (Bileghan) Bridge on the Tehran-Karaj highway in Alborz Province — the highest bridge in the Middle East. The bridge collapsed, killing 8 and wounding 95. The strike came during Sizdah Be-dar (Iranian nature holiday), when families were picnicking in parks below the structure. Iran had used this route to transport missiles from Tehran to western launch sites. The strike drew condemnation from international legal experts as potentially disproportionate.

events.title: Makram Atimi Killed — Iran's Ballistic Missile Chief
events.detail: Makram Atimi, commander of Iran's central ballistic missile battalion and assessed as the overall ballistic missile chief, was killed in an IDF strike in the Kermanshah area. Several battalion commanders from his central Iranian ballistic missile unit were also killed in the same strike. Gen. Mohammad Ali Fathali Zadeh, commander of the Fatehin Battalions (Brigadier General), was also killed on Day 34.

events.title: Iranian Missile Strikes Drone Factory in Petah Tikva — Cluster Munitions Hit Central Israel
events.detail: Iran struck an Israeli drone factory in Petah Tikva with a ballistic missile carrying a cluster munition warhead. Over 20 Iranian missiles hit Israel within a 24-hour period (April 1-2). An Iranian ballistic missile with a cluster warhead also struck near Petah Tikva city center. Additional cluster munition missiles targeted UAE (26 drones, 9 ballistic missiles intercepted), Kuwait (13 drones, 2 cruise missiles), Bahrain (10 drones, 2 missiles), Qatar, and Saudi Arabia on the same day.

events.title: Iranian Strike on Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, Jordan — US MQ-9 Destroyed
events.detail: Iran conducted a drone and missile strike on Muwaffaq Salti (Azraq) Air Base in Jordan, approximately 100 km northeast of Amman, where US fighter aircraft are based. Jordan intercepted most projectiles, but at least one penetrated defenses, damaging warehouses and ground assets including a US MQ-9 Reaper drone. Minor injuries were reported among base personnel. Jordan publicly condemned the strike.

events.title: Hezbollah Conducts 28 Attacks on IDF, Rockets Hit Kiryat Shmona
events.detail: Hezbollah conducted 28 attacks against IDF forces in southern Lebanon on April 2 — including ATGMs, mortar fire, rockets, and an IED against an IDF Merkava tank near Ainata village. 32 additional attacks targeted northern Israeli communities including Kiryat Shmona, Krayot, Shlomi, Metula, and Nahariya. Two people were injured in Kiryat Shmona, two in Biina. IDF killed 40 Hezbollah operatives in Lebanon over 24 hours.

events.title: Trump Prime-Time Address: 11,000 Targets Struck, Escalation Vowed
events.detail: President Trump delivered a prime-time national address on Day 33, claiming US strikes had "dramatically curtailed" Iranian missile and drone capability and that CENTCOM had confirmed 11,000+ total targets struck since February 28. He threatened to strike more targets over the next 2-3 weeks to "bring them back to the Stone Ages." Independent analysts including Col. Macgregor (ret.) immediately disputed the degradation claims, citing insider assessments of only 25-30% actual missile capability reduction.

events.title: Hengam Island Struck — Strait of Hormuz Naval Interdiction Zone
events.detail: US-Israeli forces struck Hengam Island in the Strait of Hormuz, causing 7 injuries and destroying buildings and docked vessels. Hengam Island is strategically critical to Iranian naval interdiction operations in the Strait. Combined forces also struck Qeshm Island port warehouses (eastern zone); satellite imagery on April 2 showed a large smoke plume. Iran stores naval assets including uncrewed surface vessels on Qeshm.

events.title: AQUA 1 Tanker Struck by Iran — QatarEnergy LNG Vessel Hit
events.detail: Iran attacked the Panamanian-flagged tanker AQUA 1, leased by QatarEnergy, 17 nautical miles northwest of Ras Laffan, Qatar. Three cruise missiles were fired; two were intercepted, one struck the hull, sustaining damage. This was the second Iranian attack on a civilian vessel in two consecutive days. US Secretary of State Rubio called the strikes "piracy."

events.title: Iran University of Science and Technology Struck — Tehran
events.detail: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Iran University of Science and Technology in Tehran on April 1, as part of broader strikes on dual-use educational and research institutions. Iran's Minister of Science stated the total number of universities struck in the war had exceeded 30. Iran threatened to retaliate by attacking Israeli and American academic facilities in the Middle East.

events.title: Mehdi Vafaei Killed — IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Underground Chief
events.detail: Mehdi Vafaei, Head of Engineering for the IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps, was killed in a targeted strike in Mahallat, Markazi Province. Vafaei was responsible for all underground storage facilities in Lebanon holding advanced Hezbollah weapons, including long-range precision missiles. His death was assessed as severely degrading Hezbollah's Lebanese weapons infrastructure management.

events.title: Kamal Kharrazi Seriously Wounded, Wife Killed
events.detail: Kamal Kharrazi, former Iranian Foreign Minister and head of Iran's Strategic Council on Foreign Relations, was seriously injured when his Tehran home was struck by an airstrike. His wife was killed. Pepe Escobar reported: "After Kharrazi, there will be no negotiators left. It will be IRGC hardcore. That's it." The strike was seen by independent analysts as severely reducing the pool of moderate Iranian officials willing to engage in diplomacy.
events.sourceText: Wikipedia (2026 Iran War) / Pepe Escobar / Asia Times

events.title: Politico: Pentagon Running Out of Strategically Important Targets
events.detail: Politico reported April 2 that Pentagon officials privately warned the US was "running out of strategically important targets to hit in Iran." Key remaining Iranian missile stockpiles are assessed to be located in hardened underground bunkers inaccessible without a ground invasion. The assessment highlighted the limits of an air-only campaign against Iran's distributed, deeply buried military architecture.

events.title: Macgregor: Only 25-30% of Iranian Missile Capability Degraded
events.detail: Col. Douglas Macgregor (Ret.) stated on Judging Freedom: "What I am hearing from people who are on the inside that I trust is that we have perhaps reduced Iran's ballistic missile capability from 100% down to perhaps 70%, not the near total destruction being claimed publicly." Macgregor argued only 25-30% genuine degradation had been achieved — contradicting CENTCOM and Hegseth's public claims of 90% degradation. He also noted the US Navy is "parked hundreds of miles south of the Gulf because it does not want to be sunk."

events.title: Artesh 55th Airborne Brigade HQ Struck — Shiraz
events.detail: US-Israeli combined forces struck the Artesh 55th Airborne Brigade headquarters in Shiraz, Fars Province. The 55th Airborne is Iran's primary rapid-deployment airborne unit. Strike destroyed command infrastructure and a weapons depot. IRGC-affiliated media confirmed the hit. IDF simultaneously struck the Artesh Joint Staff offices in northern Tehran.

events.title: Three Major Steel Factories Struck — Dual-Use Targeting
events.detail: US-Israeli forces struck three Iranian steel plants assessed as dual-use for ballistic missile production: Sefid Dasht industrial zone (Boroujen, Charmahal-Bakhtiari), Khuzestan Steel Company (Ahvaz), and Mobarakeh Steel Company (Mobarakeh, Isfahan). ISW assessed the strikes as intended to degrade missile-component manufacturing capacity and apply economic pressure simultaneously.

events.title: IRGC Commander Eshaghi Killed — Armed Forces General Staff
events.detail: Eshaghi, an adviser to the Armed Forces General Staff chief, was killed in a targeted airstrike on Day 32. He had been sanctioned by the US Treasury in February 2025 for his role in facilitating illicit Iranian oil sales to China. Mohammad Sadeghi, an IRGC Aerospace Force al-Ghadir Missile Command engineering officer responsible for constructing underground missile tunnels, was also killed the same day.

events.title: Kuwait International Airport Fuel Tanks Hit by Iranian Drone
events.detail: An Iranian drone struck fuel storage tanks at Kuwait International Airport, causing significant fire and damage. No casualties were reported. The strike demonstrated Iran's continued ability to hit civilian infrastructure in Gulf states despite sustained degradation of its drone inventory. Kuwait scrambled F/A-18s in response.

events.title: Iran Cluster-Munition Salvo Hits Bnei Brak, Central Israel
events.detail: Iran launched a five-salvo missile campaign starting 2:00 PM ET on March 31 (continuing through April 1). The fourth salvo — 10 missiles, one of the largest single salvos since the war began — included cluster munition warheads that fell in Bnei Brak and two other central Israel sites, injuring at least 10 civilians. Iran's use of cluster munitions in ~70% of missiles targeting Israel by late March raised war crimes concerns under international law.

events.title: Hezbollah Fires 71 Attacks on Israel and IDF — Day 32
events.detail: Hezbollah conducted 71 attacks against northern Israeli communities and IDF positions between 2:00 PM ET March 31 and through April 1. Four FPV drone strikes targeted IDF armored vehicles in southern Lebanon, with footage posted to Hezbollah's Telegram channel. Three Amal Movement members were killed by IDF strikes in southern Lebanon in the same period.

events.title: Islamic Resistance in Iraq Claims 23 Drone Attacks
events.detail: Iraq-based Iran-aligned militias claimed 23 drone attacks against US and partner bases across the region on Day 32. Targets included Baghdad International Airport (two drone strikes; first intercepted, second struck a diplomatic support center with no casualties) and US Qasrak Base in Hasakah, Syria. Saraya Awliya al-Dam separately claimed 5 additional attacks against US bases.

events.title: US Gas Prices Cross $4/Gallon for First Time Since 2022
events.detail: The national average US gasoline price reached $4.02/gallon on March 31, crossing the $4 threshold for the first time since August 2022. The price had been $2.98/gallon before the war began on February 28 — a 35% increase in 31 days. Diesel hit $5.45/gallon. California prices surpassed $5.00/gallon. EV listing interest on CarGurus surged 31% from early to late March as drivers sought alternatives.

events.title: Bloomberg: 2,400 Interceptors Expended — Coalition Nearing Stockpile Limit
events.detail: Bloomberg reported that coalition forces had expended at least 2,400 interceptor missiles — approaching known prewar stockpile levels — in defending against Iran's sustained drone and missile campaign. The figure underscored Iran's deliberate attrition strategy: using cheap Shahed-136 drones (~$20,000–50,000 each) to exhaust high-cost US/allied interceptors ($1–3M per Patriot PAC-3 shot). The US was rushing production orders but industrial base ramp-up would take 12–18 months.

events.title: Iran: 1,252+ Ballistic Missiles and 2,328+ Drones Fired in 31 Days
events.detail: Aggregate wartime launch data showed Iran had fired 1,252+ ballistic missiles and 2,328+ drones/UAVs across the 31-day conflict. The most heavily targeted country by ballistic missiles was the UAE (357), followed by Israel (220), Kuwait (178), Bahrain (102), and Qatar (101). IDF claimed ~330 of 470 Iranian missile launchers were destroyed or inoperable, but Iran's underground missile cities remained the primary production and storage reserve.

events.title: Total Israeli Casualties: 29 Killed, 5,768 Injured From Iranian Strikes
events.detail: Comprehensive casualty figures for Israeli victims of Iranian strikes after 31 days: 29 civilians and soldiers killed, 5,768 injured. Iranian cluster munitions — used in ~70% of missiles targeting Israel by late March — were responsible for a disproportionate share of casualties due to wide-area fragmentation. Lebanon war casualties remained separate; IDF ground operations in Lebanon had produced 3 additional soldiers killed.

events.title: Houthis Threaten Bab al-Mandab Closure — Second Chokepoint Warning
events.detail: The Indian Express and Al Jazeera published analysis of the Houthi threat to close Bab al-Mandab Strait, which handles ~10% of global seaborne trade including significant European energy imports. Combined with the Hormuz closure, a Bab al-Mandab blockade would simultaneously strangle Persian Gulf energy exports AND Red Sea/Suez trade routes — a scenario described by analysts as potentially triggering a global depression rather than a recession.
events.sourceText: Indian Express / Al Jazeera / Anadolu Agency

events.title: 27 Days of Near-Total Iranian Internet Blackout
events.detail: Iran had experienced 27+ straight days of near-complete internet blackout, with connectivity at 1–4% of normal. Despite this, the Iranian government maintained operational continuity, missile launches continued, and the IRGC's Electronic Operations Room coordinated cyber and information operations through externally-based Iranian-aligned operatives. The internet blackout prevented independent documentation of civilian casualties and internal conditions inside Iran.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Draws Mass Crowds — Rally-Round-Flag Effect Holds
events.detail: Massive crowds turned out in support of newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei in Tehran and other Iranian cities on March 28-30. Scott Ritter: "There has never been more support in Iran for the Iranian government. And the more we bomb Iran, the stronger that support gets." No significant defections were documented within the IRGC through Day 31, validating pre-war Iranian assessments that decapitation strikes would strengthen rather than weaken regime cohesion.

events.title: Houthis Fire Second Salvo — Cruise Missiles and Drones at Israel
events.detail: The Houthis fired their second strike at Israel within 24 hours, including cruise missiles and drones targeting Israeli military positions. Houthi Deputy Information Minister Mansour stated closing Bab al-Mandab Strait was "among our options" depending on US/Israeli escalation, warning the campaign was "conducted in phases." Closing Bab al-Mandab alongside Hormuz would create two simultaneous global shipping chokepoints trapping an enormous share of global energy trade.
events.sourceText: Al Jazeera / Anadolu Agency / Indian Express

events.title: Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Industrial Zone Struck in Negev
events.detail: Iranian missiles struck the Ne'ot Hovav chemical industrial zone in Israel's Negev desert — one of the most sensitive industrial sites in Israel, housing chemical processing plants and hazardous materials facilities. The strike raised concerns about potential toxic release and represented Iran's systematic targeting of Israeli critical industrial infrastructure alongside military targets.

events.title: Al Jazeera Summary: 12 MQ-9 Reapers Lost, 21 Ships Struck, 332 US Wounded
events.detail: Al Jazeera published a comprehensive one-month war damage assessment: Iran had caused the loss or destruction of 12 MQ-9 Reaper drones (at ~$30M each), 21 commercial ships had been struck or seized in the Strait of Hormuz, and US wounded had reached approximately 332 service members. US confirmed killed stood at 13–15 service members. At least 2,400 coalition interceptors had been expended, approaching known prewar stockpile levels.

events.title: Lebanon: 1,000 Killed, 1 Million Displaced in 27 Days of Fighting
events.detail: One month after Hezbollah resumed its campaign, Lebanon had suffered 1,000+ killed (civilians and fighters combined), 2,500+ injured, and approximately 1 million displaced — nearly 20% of Lebanon's entire population. Three IDF soldiers had been killed in ground combat operations in southern Lebanon. INSS assessed Hezbollah was "waging a war of survival" with the strategic goal of eventually forcing Israeli withdrawal.
events.sourceText: Wikipedia 2026 Lebanon War / INSS / J Street

events.title: Houthis Open New Front — Ballistic Missiles Target Southern Israel
events.detail: The Houthis entered the Iran war on March 28 — exactly one month after it began — firing ballistic missiles targeting "sensitive military locations" in southern Israel, with sirens activated near Beersheba and the Negev nuclear research facility. IDF confirmed intercepts with no casualties. Houthi Deputy Information Minister Mohammed Mansour stated: "We are in joint coordination with our brothers in Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq."

events.title: Iraqi PMF Convoy Enters Khorramshahr, Iran
events.detail: A convoy of Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) entered Khorramshahr, Iran — marking a significant escalation and signaling Iran was preparing to use PMF forces on its own territory in the event of a US ground invasion attempt. The PMF entry was widely interpreted as Iran activating its full Axis of Resistance mobilization plan, positioning foreign allied fighters inside Iranian territory for the first time.

events.title: Islamic Resistance in Iraq Conducts 41 Attacks in One Day
events.detail: Iraqi militias affiliated with the Islamic Resistance in Iraq conducted 41 separate drone and missile operations against US bases in a single 24-hour period — nearly double the previous daily record. Targets included Victory Base (Baghdad), Erbil, and forward operating positions in eastern Syria. UK Defence Secretary John Healey stated it "would be unsurprising to consider that Putin's hidden hand influences some of Iran's tactics" in reference to the coordinated attack patterns.
events.sourceText: Anadolu Agency / Middle East Monitor / Al Jazeera

events.title: Drop Site News: Iran's Ceasefire Framework Revealed
events.detail: Drop Site News (Jeremy Scahill) obtained Iran's ceasefire framework submitted to mediators. Key conditions: (1) end to the Lebanon war as a precondition; (2) compensation for Iranian civilian and infrastructure destruction; (3) elimination of all sanctions; (4) end to US military presence in the region. Iran rejected the US's 15-point counter-framework as "disingenuous." Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf stated: "Iran could not be forced into submission."

events.title: Iran Strikes Prince Sultan AB Again — E-3 AWACS and Tankers Hit, 15 Soldiers Wounded
events.detail: A second major Iranian missile strike on Prince Sultan Air Base hit additional KC-135 Stratotankers and struck an E-3 Sentry AWACS airborne warning and control aircraft — a strategic asset valued at approximately $270 million and critical to coordinating US air operations. 15 US soldiers were wounded (5 critically). Iran's Ibrahim Zaghari claimed one refueling aircraft was "destroyed" and three others "rendered inoperable." Satellite imagery released by Iran's Press TV showed multiple aircraft wrecked at the airbase, though US maintained a media blackout on base damage.

events.title: Brent Crude at $110+ — Trump Lifts Russia Sanctions to Offset Energy Crisis
events.detail: Brent crude remained above $110/barrel (down from the $126 peak but still historically extreme). Trump was forced to lift select sanctions on Russian oil exports to help mitigate US domestic energy prices — effectively giving Russia financial breathing room as a consequence of Iran's Hormuz strategy. Markets.FinancialContent cited analysts calling the move "Iran's biggest indirect geopolitical victory."

events.title: NYT: Iran Hormuz Toll Legislation Moving Through Parliament
events.detail: The New York Times reported Iran's parliament was actively working to formalize the Hormuz transit fee system into legislation, signaling the toll regime was not a temporary wartime measure but a permanent strategic posture. Nearly 3,000 vessels were waiting to transit as of late March. 26 vessels had already transited under the IRGC "toll booth" system, paying up to $2 million per voyage.

events.title: Russia Formally Admits Military Equipment Supplied to Iran — Lavrov
events.detail: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov confirmed in an interview with French television: "We have a technical-military cooperation agreement, and within that framework, we have provided military equipment to Iran." Lavrov denied providing intelligence on US positions but added tellingly: "Tehran itself has precise knowledge of US positions and does not need us." This directly contradicted the Kremlin's March 5 denial.

events.title: CNBC Analysis: Iran's BeiDou Use Explains Improved Accuracy
events.detail: CNBC published analysis by SandboxAQ CEO Jack Hidary confirming that Iran's use of China's BeiDou satellite navigation system explained the marked improvement in Iranian missile accuracy compared to the June 2025 12-Day War. BeiDou's encrypted military signals are less vulnerable to Western jamming, provide sub-meter accuracy under optimal conditions, and include a short-message feature allowing mid-flight missile redirection up to 2,000 km.

events.title: Iraqi Militias Conduct 23 Attacks in 24 Hours Against US Bases
events.detail: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq conducted 23 separate drone and rocket operations in 24 hours against US bases across the region — a new daily intensity record at the time. Targets included Victory Base Complex (Baghdad), Erbil (northern Iraq), and bases in Syria. The militias had conducted 200+ total claimed attacks since February 28. US officials noted the attack rate was accelerating as Iranian coordination improved.
events.sourceText: Anadolu Agency / Middle East Monitor / National Interest

events.title: Reuters: Turkey Serving as Back-Channel Messenger Between Iran and US
events.detail: Reuters confirmed that Turkey was conveying messages between Iran and the United States, acting as the primary back-channel in the absence of direct diplomatic communication. Turkey's FM Hakan Fidan had declared Israel the "primary responsible party" for the war while simultaneously facilating de-escalation messaging. Turkey blocked US proposals to use Kurdish forces against Iran and was serving as the key diplomatic node for any eventual negotiated settlement.

events.title: Atlantic Council: China and Russia Supplying Iran Through Supply Chains
events.detail: The Atlantic Council published detailed findings confirming China and Russia were supplying Iran through supply chain channels — including chemical precursors for rocket fuel (sodium perchlorate picked up at China's Zhuhai port by sanctioned Iranian vessels), Chinese electronics with inertial sensors and navigation modules for missile guidance, and Russian-made improved Shahed drones reversing the original flow of drone technology.

events.title: Iran Begins Charging $2M "Passage Fees" at Hormuz — Bloomberg Confirms
events.detail: Bloomberg confirmed Iran had begun charging transit fees of up to $2 million per voyage for commercial vessels seeking safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran was effectively exercising sovereignty over the world's most critical maritime chokepoint. Approved nations: China, Russia, Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, and Iraq. US and Israeli-linked ships were denied passage. Ships required IRGC vetting and were escorted by Iranian escort boats through territorial waters near Larak Island.
events.sourceText: Bloomberg / Al Jazeera / NYT / Marine Insight

events.title: Philippines Declares State of Emergency Over Fuel Shortage
events.detail: The Philippines declared a national state of emergency driven by acute fuel shortages resulting from the Hormuz closure. Zimbabwe, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nigeria, and Vietnam also faced severe fuel shortages. The US was facing a nearly 25% fertilizer shortfall as urea supply chains collapsed. Petrochemical supply chains globally were disrupted as Iran held approximately 35% of global urea trade via Hormuz.

events.title: CNA Analysis: China, Russia Partnership Tested — Both Benefiting
events.detail: CNA (Center for Naval Analyses) published analysis showing the Iran conflict was testing but not breaking the China-Russia partnership. Russia was profiting from oil price spikes funding its Ukraine war, while China was insulated by 4-month strategic oil reserves and coal alternatives. Trump was forced to lift sanctions on Russian oil to mitigate domestic energy prices, effectively giving Russia financial breathing room through Iranian-created market disruption.

events.title: Clemson Study Exposes IRGC Social Media Influence Network — 61 Accounts
events.detail: Clemson University researchers published findings of an IRGC-affiliated influence network of at least 61 accounts across X/Twitter, Instagram, and Bluesky. The accounts had previously operated as UK/Scottish independence influence accounts before switching to pro-Islamic Republic war propaganda within 24 hours of Feb 28. They produced 59,403 original posts reaching potentially millions of users. Most were suspended after the study's publication.

events.title: Brent Crude Peaks at $126/Barrel — Record High for Conflict
events.detail: Brent crude oil reached its recorded conflict peak of $126/barrel on March 22. The oil price surge was creating $200+ billion in windfall revenue for Russia, which Trump had been forced to unilaterally lift some Russian oil sanctions to mitigate US domestic energy prices. Semafor reported that supply shocks were giving China and Russia significant geopolitical leverage.

events.title: Dimona Nuclear Research Center Struck — 78+ Injured
events.detail: Iranian missiles struck near Israel's Dimona Negev Nuclear Research Center in the Negev desert, injuring 78+ people. The same attack hit the nearby town of Arad, injuring an additional 116+ people. The strikes on or near Israel's nuclear research infrastructure raised immediate proliferation concerns and international alarm. The IDF confirmed the impact locations but provided limited damage assessment.

events.title: Brent Crude Peaks at $126/Barrel — Dubai Crude Hits $166.80
events.detail: Brent crude oil reached a peak recorded price of $126/barrel on March 22 (having begun its approach on March 21). Dubai crude — the benchmark for Asian buyers — hit $166.80/barrel as Asian importers competed for available non-Hormuz supply. Barclays estimated sustained $100+ oil reduced global GDP by 0.2 percentage points and raised inflation by 0.7 percentage points per sustained month.

events.title: CNN Investigation: Iran's Underground Missile Cities Largely Intact
events.detail: CNN published a major investigative piece with satellite imagery and expert analysis confirming Iran's deep underground missile cities were largely intact despite US strikes. The Imam Hussein Base (Yazd Province) was built 440–500 meters into the Sheru granite formation — one of the hardest rock types on Earth. Underground rail connects assembly halls to multiple tunnel exits. Scott Ritter: "The buildings being bombed are empty buildings. Iran has been preparing for this conflict for 20 years."

events.title: IEA Orders Largest Emergency SPR Release in History — 400 Million Barrels
events.detail: The International Energy Agency ordered the largest emergency strategic petroleum reserve release in history: approximately 400 million barrels — more than double any previous release, representing one-third of total IEA member stockpiles combined. The IEA described the Hormuz crisis as "the greatest global energy security challenge in history." The release was partially offset by continued refusal of most vessels to transit the strait.

events.title: BAZAN Group Oil Refinery in Haifa Struck
events.detail: Iranian ballistic missiles struck the BAZAN Group oil refinery complex in Haifa — Israel's primary oil refinery — causing significant damage and fires. The strike was part of Iran's declared strategy to impose "eye-for-an-eye" economic damage on Israel mirroring the economic pain the Hormuz closure was inflicting on global markets.

events.title: IRGC Claims F-35 Engaged by Iranian Air Defense — Emergency Landing
events.detail: The IRGC released footage it claimed showed Iranian air defense systems engaging a US F-35 stealth fighter, which subsequently made an emergency landing. US officials declined to confirm or deny the incident. If confirmed, it would be the first combat damage sustained by an operational F-35 from a non-peer adversary air defense system. The footage was widely circulated on Iranian state media.

events.title: Netanyahu Claims Iran Cannot Produce Ballistic Missiles — Iran Contradicts
events.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu publicly claimed that Iran could no longer produce ballistic missiles due to Israeli and US strikes on production facilities. The IRGC responded the following day, directly contradicting the claim and stating "Our missile industry deserves a perfect score... even under wartime conditions we continue missile production." CENTCOM's simultaneous targeting of bulldozers clearing tunnel entrances suggested the underground network had survived primary strikes.

events.title: Iran Strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG Complex — 17% Capacity Loss
events.detail: Iranian missiles struck Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City LNG complex, causing a 17% reduction in Qatar's LNG production capacity. Damage repair was estimated to take 3–5 years. QatarEnergy declared force majeure on all LNG export contracts. Qatar supplies approximately 20% of global LNG; European countries that had shifted from Russian pipeline gas to Qatari LNG faced an immediate supply crisis. Asian LNG spot prices ultimately increased over 140% above pre-war levels.

events.title: GCC Food Emergency: 70% of Imports Disrupted, Prices Up 40–120%
events.detail: Gulf Cooperation Council states — which rely on Hormuz for over 80% of their caloric intake — declared a growing food supply emergency by mid-March. 70% of food imports were disrupted. Consumer food prices across the Gulf rose 40–120%. Retailers including Lulu Retail began emergency airlift-shipping of staple foods. India shut down 3 of its urea plants due to gas shortages; Bangladesh closed 4 of its 5 fertilizer plants.

events.title: Ramat Gan Apartment Hit by Cluster Munition — 2 Elderly Civilians Killed
events.detail: An Iranian cluster munition warhead struck a residential apartment building in Ramat Gan (Greater Tel Aviv), killing 2 Israeli civilians in their 70s and injuring multiple others. By March 17, the IDF reported approximately 50% of all Iranian missiles targeting Israel were carrying cluster warheads. Al Jazeera published analysis of how cluster munitions were penetrating Israeli defenses.

events.title: Palo Alto Unit 42: 60+ Hacktivist Groups Active in Iran Cyber Campaign
events.detail: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 published a comprehensive analysis confirming 60+ hacktivist groups were active in Iran's coordinated cyber campaign, including pro-Russian groups collaborating with Iranian state-sponsored actors. The Stryker Corporation (US medical technology firm) suffered a significant hack; its online ordering system remained non-operational for weeks. The 313 Team (Islamic Cyber Resistance in Iraq) targeted Kuwait Armed Forces and government websites.

events.title: IDF Launches Ground Operations in Southern Lebanon
events.detail: The Israel Defense Forces launched ground operations into southern Lebanon, opening a second active ground front alongside the air campaign against Iran. Hezbollah had fired 1,000+ projectiles into Israel since resuming attacks on March 2. The Chatham House assessment noted: "Any Israeli occupation of southern Lebanon will work to Hezbollah's advantage" by reinvigorating the group domestically and politically.

events.title: EU Leaders Formally Reject Military Role in Hormuz
events.detail: Following EU foreign ministers' meetings, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas stated: "Nobody wants to actively engage in this war." The UK, Japan, Australia, Germany, Spain, Italy, France, and Greece all explicitly declined Trump's requests for military assistance in the Strait of Hormuz. Germany's Defense Minister stated bluntly: "This is not our conflict. We did not initiate it." This was a major diplomatic setback for the US coalition-building effort.

events.title: Shipping Insurance Rates Hit 5% of Vessel Value
events.detail: Hull war risk insurance for Persian Gulf transits reached 5% of vessel value by March 16 — equivalent to $5 million to insure a $100 million tanker per voyage. Bloomberg reported the rate represented a 10,000%+ increase from pre-war levels. The Standard P&I Club and London P&I Club both formally withdrew coverage from the Persian Gulf war zone.

events.title: Axios: Iran Cyber Playbook Targets US Critical Infrastructure
events.detail: Axios published an investigation into Iran's multi-domain cyber campaign, confirming state-sponsored actors had installed backdoors in several US technology firms as early as late February. Symantec and Carbon Black researchers confirmed the pre-positioned access. Iranian state media published a list of major US tech companies as potential escalation targets. Poland reported a thwarted cyberattack on a nuclear research facility connected to Iranian actors.

events.title: Trump: Russia "Might Be Helping Them a Bit" — Fox News Interview
events.detail: In a Fox News interview, President Trump acknowledged that Russia "might be helping them a bit," referring to Russian military support for Iran. Iran's FM responded by stating Russia's military partnership with Tehran was "good." Trump's admission contradicted his earlier framing of the war as a straightforward US-Iran bilateral conflict and raised questions about the broader geopolitical dimensions of the conflict.

events.title: Iranian Missiles Damage 5 KC-135 Stratotankers at Prince Sultan AB
events.detail: A major Iranian ballistic missile strike on Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia) damaged 5 KC-135 Stratotanker refueling aircraft, each worth approximately $60–75 million. US officials confirmed the strike. President Trump publicly stated 4 of the 5 had "virtually no damage," contradicting statements from base personnel. The KC-135 is the primary aerial refueling aircraft enabling long-range strike missions against Iran.

events.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Suffers Major Fire — Sails to Crete
events.detail: The aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) suffered a serious fire aboard, forcing a halt to strike operations and a sailing for Crete, Greece for emergency repairs. The US stated it was a non-combat fire; Iran's allies claimed credit. The Ford's temporary absence reduced the US carrier strike package operating in the eastern Mediterranean at a critical operational phase.

events.title: Al Jazeera: Five Vessels Attacked in Hormuz — Drone Boats and Sea Mines Confirmed
events.detail: Al Jazeera published a compilation of five confirmed ship attack incidents involving Iranian drone boats and sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Nearly 3,000 commercial vessels were now waiting to transit the strait as normal daily traffic of ~120 ships dropped to near-standstill. S&P Global Commodity Insights tracked the vessel backlog. Global food price indices began rising sharply as fertilizer and grain shipments stalled.

events.title: UNSC Resolution 2817: Condemns Iran — US Vetoes Russian Ceasefire Resolution
events.detail: Bahrain (on behalf of 6 Gulf states + Jordan) introduced UNSC Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's retaliatory strikes — but notably NOT the original US-Israel strikes. Vote: 13-0 in favor with China and Russia abstaining. Russia simultaneously proposed a separate resolution calling for an end to the entire war; the United States vetoed it. Iran framed the veto as proof that the "rules-based order" was structurally rigged against non-Western states.

events.title: Al Jazeera: Iran Using BeiDou Satellite Navigation System
events.detail: Al Jazeera published a detailed investigation confirming Iran had switched from GPS to China's BeiDou satellite navigation system for missile and drone guidance. Former French intelligence chief Alain Juillet and CIA Director John Ratcliffe both confirmed BeiDou access. Key advantages: encrypted military signals less vulnerable to Western jamming, sub-meter accuracy, mid-flight redirection capability up to 2,000 km, and immunity to Israeli GPS spoofing that worked against Iran in the June 2025 12-Day War.
events.sourceText: Al Jazeera / Small Wars Journal / CNBC

events.title: 3 Cargo Ships Damaged in Hormuz USV and Mine Attacks
events.detail: USNI News and Reuters confirmed 3 cargo ships were damaged in suspected Iranian attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, involving a combination of naval mines and Unmanned Surface Vehicle (USV) drone boats. Five vessels had been attacked or struck in total up to this date. Iran had issued formal warnings that any vessel transiting without IRGC authorization would face "severe action."

events.title: US Costs Reach $16.5 Billion Through Day 12
events.detail: Pentagon internal briefings tallied total war costs at $16.5 billion through the first 12 days of the conflict, including munitions expended, equipment damaged or destroyed, and operational costs for the carrier strike groups, bomber sorties, and logistics surge. The Pentagon was preparing a $200 billion supplemental budget request to Congress. Interceptor stockpile depletion was described by officials as a "serious concern."

events.title: Oil Production Collapse Reaches 10 Million Barrels/Day
events.detail: Global oil production disruption reached at least 10 million barrels per day by March 10-12, as the full Hormuz closure effect rippled through markets. The IEA convened an emergency session and began planning its largest-ever strategic petroleum reserve release. GCC states — which rely on Hormuz for 80%+ of food imports — faced a growing grocery supply emergency.

events.title: Iran Announces Strategic Shift to 1,000 kg+ Payload Missiles Only
events.detail: A senior IRGC commander publicly declared Iran was shifting from mass-saturation tactics (Shahed drones + light missiles to exhaust interceptors) to exclusively firing missiles with payloads of 1,000 kg or more — including Khorramshahr (1,500+ kg warhead), Emad (750 kg), and Fattah hypersonic variants. AFP described subsequent attacks as the "most intense and heaviest since the war began." This shift was designed to physically destroy hardened targets rather than deplete interceptor stocks.

events.title: NYT: Iranian Food Prices Surge Inside Iran
events.detail: The New York Times reported on food shortages and price surges inside Iran, with egg prices nearly doubling in two weeks and bakeries operating with unusually thin stocks. Iran had imposed a near-complete internet blackout from Day 1 (connectivity dropped to 1–4% of normal). 70,000+ Afghan refugees returned from outside Iran to inside Iran in the first two weeks — likely as conditions abroad deteriorated for Afghan migrant workers.

events.title: Drop Site News: Iran Claims "Most Tactical Aims Achieved"
events.detail: Drop Site News (Jeremy Scahill) published reporting based on a direct communication from a senior Iranian official, who stated Iran had "achieved most of its tactical aims against US military infrastructure" by early March. Iran's strategic logic, per the official: "We want to cause enough pain to the world economy that any nation that thinks of attacking Iran again knows these are the consequences."

events.title: Yehud Strike: 2 Israeli Workers Killed by Cluster Submunitions
events.detail: An Iranian ballistic missile carrying a cluster munition warhead struck the Yehud industrial area east of Tel Aviv, killing 2 Israeli workers by fragmenting submunitions over a wide area. IDF assessed ~50% of Iranian missiles targeting Israel at this stage were carrying cluster warheads, raising alarms about civilian protection under international humanitarian law. Al Jazeera published a detailed cluster munition analysis.

events.title: Soufan Center Publishes Analysis of Iran's Mosaic Defense Doctrine
events.detail: The Soufan Center published an analysis of Iran's "Mosaic Defense" doctrine — 31 separate IRGC regional commands each with independent intelligence, weapons stockpiles, and pre-set general orders. Iran's FM Araghchi had admitted on March 1: "Our military units are now independent and somewhat isolated, and they are acting based on general instructions given in advance." This explained strikes on Oman — autonomous units that fired without central confirmation.

events.title: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington Dies from March 1 Wounds
events.detail: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington died from wounds sustained in the March 1 drone and missile attacks on US bases, becoming one of the confirmed US service member deaths tallied in the conflict. US casualties at this stage stood at approximately 8–9 killed and over 100 wounded. The Pentagon issued a statement expressing condolences but maintained strict information controls on base damage assessments.

events.title: Oil Production Drops 6.7 Million Barrels/Day
events.detail: Global oil production had dropped by 6.7 million barrels per day as of March 8-10, driven by the Hormuz closure and market panic. Iraq suspended oil terminal operations after two tankers were struck. The UAE and Saudi Arabia were diverting some production to overland pipelines (Fujairah bypass, East-West pipeline), but these could handle only a fraction of normal Hormuz throughput.

events.title: Assembly of Experts Appoints Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader
events.detail: Iran's Assembly of Experts convened and appointed Mojtaba Khamenei — son of the assassinated Supreme Leader — as the new Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic, just 8 days after his father's death. All IRGC commanders and senior officials immediately pledged allegiance. The speed of succession and absence of any significant defection within the IRGC demonstrated the regime's structural resilience.

events.title: Brent Crude Crosses $100/Barrel — Hormuz Closure Confirmed
events.detail: Brent crude oil crossed $100/barrel as the Hormuz closure was formally confirmed by shipping industry sources. The Strait carries ~20% of global oil (13 million barrels/day), ~20–25% of global LNG, and ~35% of global urea. The IEA described the situation as the "greatest global energy security challenge in history." Asian LNG spot prices began their climb toward 140%+ above pre-war levels.

events.title: Iranian Red Crescent: 65 Schools, 32 Medical Facilities Targeted
events.detail: The Iranian Red Crescent published an interim damage assessment showing US/Israeli strikes had hit 65 schools, 32 medical facilities, and more than 10,000 civilian sites. A total of 6,668+ civilian dwelling units had been struck by March 7. HRANA (Iranian human rights monitors) separately documented 3,114 total deaths including 1,354 civilians by March 17.

events.title: Maritime Insurance Rates Hit 3% of Vessel Value
events.detail: Hull war risk insurance for Hormuz transits reached 3% of vessel value — equivalent to $3 million to insure a $100 million tanker per voyage. Major marine war risk reinsurers tripled premiums or canceled coverage entirely. Lloyd's of London syndicates issued formal war zone declarations covering the entire Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, and Red Sea approaches.

events.title: Hezbollah Rockets Strike Golan Heights and Haifa
events.detail: Hezbollah launched rockets and artillery toward the Golan Heights and Haifa, expanding its strike envelope. 5 Israeli soldiers suffered severe injuries from a Hezbollah projectile near the Lebanese border. By this date UNIFIL had confirmed 210+ total projectiles fired from Lebanon since March 2.

events.title: Kremlin Claims Iran Has "Not Requested" Russian Arms
events.detail: The Kremlin officially stated Iran had "not requested Russian arms supplies" — a position that would be directly contradicted by FM Lavrov's later public admission. CIA Director John Ratcliffe had already confirmed Russia was sharing intelligence with Iran. The Kremlin statement was widely viewed as diplomatic cover; Russian-made improved Shahed drones were already being documented in theater.

events.title: US Fires 850 Tomahawk Missiles in First 6 Days — $11.3B Cost
events.detail: The Pentagon's first 6-day cost assessment totaled $11.3 billion. The US had already fired approximately 850 Tomahawk cruise missiles (at ~$2.5M each), with the Washington Post reporting remaining inventory was "alarmingly low." China and Russia both flagged the US depletion as a strategic vulnerability. The Pentagon began emergency planning for a $200 billion supplemental budget request to Congress.

events.title: WSJ Exposes Iran's Vast Underground Missile Cities
events.detail: The Wall Street Journal published a major investigative report revealing Iran's network of deep underground "missile cities" largely impervious to conventional strikes. The Imam Hussein Base in Yazd Province was built into the Sheru granite formation at 440–500 meters depth. The system featured underground rail connecting assembly halls, storage depots, and multiple tunnel exits on different mountainsides — and had been "only fully activated in 2026."

events.title: Hezbollah Launches Drones at Israeli Oil and Gas Infrastructure
events.detail: Hezbollah launched coordinated drone and missile salvos targeting Israeli military bases and oil/gas infrastructure along the northern coast. Anti-tank guided missile fire caused severe injuries to 5 Israeli soldiers near the Lebanese border. The targeting of energy infrastructure marked Hezbollah's alignment with Iran's strategic goal of imposing economic costs on Israel.

events.title: Handala Hack Compromises Israeli Energy Company and Jordan Fuel Systems
