events.detail: MOIS-linked hacktivist group Handala Hack claimed successful intrusions into an Israeli energy company's operational networks and Jordan fuel distribution systems. Separately, the FAD Team/Fatimiyoun deployed wiper malware and claimed access to SCADA/PLC control systems of 24+ Israeli security company devices. The Cyber Islamic Resistance umbrella collective claimed to compromise Israeli payment infrastructure.
events.sourceText: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 / Axios / Industrial Cyber

events.title: Hezbollah Fires Missiles at Ramat David Airbase and Camp Yitzhak
events.detail: Hezbollah fired missiles and drones at Ramat David Airbase, the Meron monitoring base, and Camp Yitzhak in northern Israel. The strikes marked a significant escalation — targeting Israeli Air Force infrastructure rather than just border communities. Anti-tank guided missile fire from Lebanese positions caused the first Israeli injuries of the Lebanon front.

events.title: IRGC: "Final Stages" of Selecting New Supreme Leader
events.detail: The IRGC announced it was in "final stages" of selecting a new Supreme Leader, just 3 days after Khamenei's death. Five senior officials and analysts cited by Reuters warned against assuming imminent collapse, noting "Iran has been structured to endure loss of leader." The Atlantic Council observed: "The Iranian system is larger than any individual — Khamenei's removal could fortify the regime."

events.title: Shipping Insurance Rates Surge 1,000%+ in Days
events.detail: War risk hull insurance for vessels transiting the Persian Gulf surged from 0.025–0.05% of vessel value to ~1–1.5% within the first days of the war — a 1,000%+ increase. Insuring a $100 million tanker went from ~$25,000 to ~$1.5 million per voyage. Major P&I clubs including American Club, Gard, and Skuld began withdrawing war risk coverage. VLCC rates from the Middle East to China surged 94% in a single day to $423,736/day.

events.title: GPS Daily Interference Events Double to 672
events.detail: Daily GPS interference events in the Persian Gulf region doubled from approximately 350 at war start to 672 by March 2. New jamming zones surged from 21 to 38 in 24 hours. Multiple parties were jamming simultaneously — Iran to confuse Western missile targeting, Gulf states to defend bases, and US Navy to protect surface vessels. Iran's switch to Chinese BeiDou guidance made it largely immune to the jamming it was itself deploying.

events.title: Hezbollah Officially Resumes Strikes From Lebanon
events.detail: Hezbollah formally resumed strikes against Israel from southern Lebanon, declaring its military return as a direct response to the assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei. Within 3 days, UNIFIL monitoring stations recorded 210+ missiles fired into northern Israel. Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem declared Hezbollah would not leave "the field of honor and resistance."

events.title: THAAD Radars Struck at UAE's Sader and Ruwais Bases
events.detail: Iranian missile strikes targeted THAAD radar vehicle sheds at Sader and Ruwais installations in the UAE, and an additional AN/TPY-2 radar at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Total THAAD radar damage in the first 48 hours valued at ~$800 million per BBC/CSIS analysis. The US was later forced to redeploy THAAD components from South Korea to replace losses.

stories.title: 2,400+ Interceptors Expended
stories.detail: As of March 30, coalition forces expended at least 2,400 interceptors — approaching known prewar stockpiles (Bloomberg). Iran's Shahed drones ($20–50k each) systematically exhausted high-cost US/allied interceptors ($1M–$3M each). Forced US to redeploy THAAD from South Korea, creating Pacific deterrence gaps.

stories.title: AIS Ship Blackouts
stories.detail: 400+ tankers stuck in Gulf. Only 4 vessels passed Hormuz with AIS on ceasefire Day 1 (Kpler). Mine chart published — ships advised to avoid danger zones.

stories.title: BGP Routing Anomalies
stories.detail: Iran's internet backbone routing near-completely disrupted since Day 1. IODA shows near-zero traffic.

stories.title: BeiDou Navigation System (China)
stories.detail: China provided Iran access to BeiDou satellite navigation — encrypted military signals less vulnerable to Western jamming, sub-meter accuracy, mid-flight redirection up to 2,000 km. Confirmed by French intel chief Alain Juillet and CIA Director Ratcliffe. Explains marked improvement in Iranian missile accuracy vs. June 2025 conflict.
stories.sourceText: Al Jazeera March 11 / Small Wars Journal / CNBC March 26

stories.title: Ceasefire Extracted After 39 Days — CRITICAL ACHIEVEMENT
stories.detail: Iran survived 39 days of sustained bombardment (13,000+ strikes, Iranian air force grounded, 150+ naval vessels destroyed) and extracted a ceasefire without capitulating on its core demands. Iran's SNSC declared the ceasefire "an enduring defeat" for Washington. Both Pakistan (primary broker) and China (last-minute pressure) intervened to preserve Iran's position. Iran entered Islamabad talks with Hormuz leverage intact and Lebanon exclusion as its primary bargaining chip. Independent analysts (Ritter, Escobar, Galbraith/The Nation): ceasefire validates Iran's strategy of strategic patience and asymmetric denial.
stories.sourceText: CENTCOM / Wikipedia / Al Jazeera / The Nation / Scott Ritter YouTube Apr 9

stories.title: China: BeiDou + Chemical Precursors
stories.detail: China provided BeiDou navigation access (dramatically improving accuracy) and — per CNA analysis — two sanctioned Iranian vessels picked up chemicals at Zhuhai port including sodium perchlorate (rocket propellant). Chinese electronics markets continued supplying inertial sensors and navigation modules for missile guidance.
stories.sourceText: CNA March 24 / Atlantic Council March 25 / Al Jazeera

stories.title: GPS Jamming Zones
stories.detail: 1,735 GPS interference events on 655 vessels (Lloyd's List Intel). 30+ jamming clusters. Data from March 7 — likely worse now.

stories.title: Hormuz De Facto Closure
stories.detail: Closed Strait of Hormuz — 20% of global oil, 20–25% of global LNG, 35% of global urea/fertilizer. ~3,000 vessels waiting as of late March (S&P Global). Normal daily traffic of 120 ships reduced to near standstill. First closure of Hormuz in modern history.

stories.title: Hormuz Toll Booth
stories.detail: Began charging $2M per voyage for IRGC-approved passage (Bloomberg, March 24). 26 ships transited under IRGC escort; vessels from China, Russia, Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Iraq approved. US/Israeli-linked ships denied. Iran parliament moved to formalize toll legislation.

stories.title: Interceptor Depletion by Country
stories.detail: Forced expenditure of 40% THAAD interceptors, 75% UAE Patriot, 87% Bahrain Patriot in 3 weeks. US cannot produce replacements fast enough. Saudi Arabia's Patriot battery at Prince Sultan AFB was among systems most strained.

stories.title: Iran Internet Status
stories.detail: ~1% of normal traffic since Day 1 (41 consecutive days). Total population-scale information blackout.

stories.title: Lebanon / Hezbollah
stories.summary: ESCALATING DESPITE CEASEFIRE — OPERATION ETERNAL DARKNESS
stories.detail:
Operation Eternal Darkness (Apr 8): Israel's largest single strike wave on Lebanon — ~50 IAF jets, ~160 munitions, 100+ Hezbollah command centers/military installations. Strikes hit Beirut southern suburbs, central areas, Beqaa Valley, southern Lebanon. 254 killed, 1,000+ injured. Five IDF divisions in Lebanon (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd). 98th Division near full control of Bint Jbail (Apr 9). 7 key bridges destroyed; southern Lebanon cut off. Hezbollah resumed rocket attacks on northern Israel (dozens fired Apr 9).

Netanyahu: "Lebanon is NOT in the ceasefire." Vance: "We never made such a commitment." Arab League: Israel "persistently seeking to sabotage" Iran ceasefire. IDF announced area south of Litani River "disconnected from Lebanon." Lebanon declared day of mourning. 1,784+ killed total in Lebanon; 1M+ displaced.

meta.keyFact: Netanyahu: "Lebanon is NOT in the ceasefire." Vance: "We never made such a commitment." Arab League: Israel "persistently seeking to sabotage" Iran ceasefire. IDF announced area south of Litani River "disconnected from Lebanon." Lebanon declared day of mourning. 1,784+ killed total in Lebanon; 1M+ displaced.

stories.title: Mosaic Defense Doctrine
stories.detail: IRGC divided into 31 autonomous regional commands, each with independent intel, stockpile, and C2. Semi-autonomous units act on pre-given instructions — decapitation strikes cannot halt operations. Iran's FM Araghchi (March 1): "Our military units are now in fact independent and somewhat isolated, and they are acting based on general instructions given in advance." Derived from study of US failures in Iraq.
stories.sourceText: Soufan Center March 9 / Al Jazeera / FM Araghchi statement

stories.title: NOTAMs / Flight Restrictions
stories.detail: Multiple TFRs over Iran, Gulf, Eastern Med. Iranian airspace fully closed.

stories.title: Russia Confirmed Arms Supply
stories.detail: Russian FM Lavrov confirmed on French TV: "We have a technical-military cooperation agreement and within that framework, we have provided military equipment to Iran." CIA Director Ratcliffe confirmed intelligence sharing. Russia supplied improved Shahed drones and shared wave-attack tactics developed in Ukraine.
stories.sourceText: Kurdistan24 March 26 / Al Jazeera March 27 / CIA Director Ratcliffe

stories.title: Sensor Network Degradation
stories.detail: Destroyed or damaged the AN/FPS-132 ($1.1B), 4 THAAD radars (~$2B), 2 SATCOM terminals, and multiple radomes across 7+ sites within first 72 hours. Systematically targeted US eyes and ears before US could fully activate defensive posture.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz
stories.detail:
Ceasefire Day 1 (Apr 8): Only 4 vessels passed with AIS active (Kpler data). Iran re-blocked after Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness. By Apr 9: oil tankers halted again per Iran's Fars News. IRGC published mine chart routing ships northward near Larak Island — implies mines in traditional Oman-side corridor. Cap: ≤15 vessels/day with IRGC approval; fees: ~$1/barrel (Iran's share for reconstruction). 400+ tankers stuck in Gulf. UK FM: Hormuz "must be toll-free."

Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

meta.keyFact: Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

stories.title: Succession in 8 Days
stories.detail: Khamenei killed Feb 28 alongside daughter, granddaughter, son-in-law. Interim Leadership Council announced March 1. Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader March 8 — just 8 days after assassination. Zero significant IRGC defections. Atlantic Council: "Khamenei's removal could fortify the regime instead of weakening it."

stories.title: Survival Under Bombardment
stories.detail: Despite >11,000 targets struck and complete US air superiority, Iran launched 7 barrages March 28–29 alone. IRGC stated March 20: "Our missile industry deserves a perfect score — even under wartime conditions we continue missile production." Directly contradicted Netanyahu's March 19 claim Iran can no longer produce ballistics.

stories.title: Tomahawk Depletion
stories.detail: 850 Tomahawk missiles fired by the US — remaining inventory described as "alarmingly low" (Washington Post). Production rate cannot replace consumption pace. This is a strategic constraint on future US force projection globally, not just in Iran theater.

stories.title: Underground Missile Cities
stories.detail: Network of missile cities carved into granite at 440–500m depth (e.g., Imam Hussein Base, Yazd Province — Sheru granite formation). Underground rail connects assembly halls, storage depots, multiple exits. "Only fully activated in 2026." CENTCOM was reduced to striking bulldozers at tunnel entrances — proving core facilities survived.
stories.sourceText: CNN March 20 / WSJ March 5 / Scott Ritter / YouTube analysis

stories.title: War Cost Imposition
stories.detail: $11.3B in first 6 days; $16.5B in first 12 days; $18B+ through Day 20. $1.4–$3.8B in equipment losses (AEI/CSIS). Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental. At $1.8B/day burn rate, production gap means costs continue accelerating.
stories.sourceText: CSIS / AEI / Pentagon briefings / Al Jazeera

stories.title: 16+ US Aircraft Destroyed
stories.detail: 3 F-15E (friendly fire induced by combat chaos), 14+ MQ-9 Reapers (9 shot down), 1 KC-135 (6 KIA), 5 KC-135s damaged, 1 E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged. Exceeds US aircraft losses in Libya and Iraq air campaigns combined.

stories.title: Cluster Munition Escalation
stories.detail: By late March, ~70% of Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Israel carry cluster warheads (up from 50% on March 10 per IDF). Cluster munition killed 2 civilians in Ramat Gan apartment (March 17); killed 2 workers in Yehud (March 9). Strategy overwhelms Iron Dome via area denial.

stories.title: Cyber Operations — 60+ Groups
stories.detail: 60+ hacktivist groups active by March 2; Electronic Operations Room coordinating Cyber Islamic Resistance, KillNet, Ghosts of Palestine, RipperSec, 313 Team (Iraq), and others. Claimed SCADA access to Israeli/Gulf industrial systems. DDoS and data-wiping operations on financial, defense, and energy infrastructure. Collaborated with pro-Russian groups.
stories.sourceText: Palo Alto Unit42 / Axios March 17 / Resecurity March 24

stories.title: Defense Stock Movement
stories.detail: Top 5 US defense contractors collectively down ~1% since war start. Production cannot replace consumption. RTX/LMT underperforming.

stories.title: EU Refused Hormuz Role
stories.detail: EU leaders explicitly rejected military involvement in Strait of Hormuz (March 16). NATO Article 5 not invoked. Only direct US allies participated. 15+ countries condemned US-Israel strikes including China, Russia, Turkey, South Africa, Brazil, Pakistan, Indonesia, Malaysia, Iraq, Syria, and multiple African nations.

stories.title: Embassy Strikes
stories.detail: Hit US Embassy Riyadh (CIA station damaged), US Embassy Kuwait, US Consulate Dubai. Unprecedented targeting of diplomatic facilities by a nation-state in a conventional conflict.

stories.title: First F-35 Hit in Combat History
stories.detail: Iranian IR-guided 358 SAM struck an F-35 during combat sortie March 16 — first time in the aircraft's history that enemy fire forced an emergency landing. Demonstrated Iranian SAM network retained capability despite sustained US SEAD campaign.

stories.title: GPS Jamming Campaign
stories.detail: 1,735 GPS interference incidents affecting 655 vessels between war start and March 3 (Lloyd's List). 1,100+ commercial vessels experienced AIS disruptions on Day 1. Iran's BeiDou switch made it largely immune to its own jamming campaign, while US/Gulf GPS-dependent systems were degraded.
stories.sourceText: Lloyd's List / CNN March 6 / CNBC March 26

stories.title: Houthi / Red Sea
stories.detail:
Apr 6: Joint Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah cruise missile + UAV operation at Eilat. 8+ Houthi attacks on Israel total. All Axis of Resistance groups announced 2-week suspension of attacks on "enemy bases" following ceasefire — but Hezbollah resumed after Lebanon strikes. Houthis have NOT resumed commercial shipping attacks.

Senior Iranian adviser Velayati (Apr 5): Bab al-Mandab can be closed "with a single signal." Houthi-Iran joint operation targeting Eilat confirms deep coordination. Bab al-Mandab remains open but explicitly threatened as escalation option.

meta.keyFact: Senior Iranian adviser Velayati (Apr 5): Bab al-Mandab can be closed "with a single signal." Houthi-Iran joint operation targeting Eilat confirms deep coordination. Bab al-Mandab remains open but explicitly threatened as escalation option.

stories.title: Intelligence Advantage
stories.detail: Precision of strikes on previously concealed US equipment demonstrated real-time targeting intelligence. Russia sharing satellite imagery of US naval/aircraft positions. UK Defence Secretary Healey: "It would be unsurprising to consider that Putin's hidden hand influences some of Iran's tactics." Trump acknowledged Russia "might be helping them a bit."
stories.sourceText: BBC / CSIS / UK Defence Secretary / Fox News (Trump)

stories.title: Multi-Country Retaliation
stories.detail: Struck targets in 14 countries simultaneously within first 6 days — largest geographic scope of Iranian military action in history. Operation True Promise 4 targeted US assets across 9 countries with ballistic missiles and drones.

stories.title: Nuclear Radiation
stories.detail: Arak reactor destroyed (IAEA). Natanz bunker-busted. Bushehr near-miss. Contamination risk from conventional strikes on nuclear material.

stories.title: Oil Price
stories.detail: Brent dropped 13% to ~$95 on ceasefire (Apr 8); rebounding to ~$97–98 Apr 9 as Hormuz re-closed. Peak: $144.42 physical (Apr 7 record). Dow surged 1,300+ pts Apr 8.

stories.title: PMF Into Iranian Territory
stories.detail: On March 29, a convoy of Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) entered Khorramshahr, Iran — indicating Iran was prepared to deploy PMF forces on its own soil if a US ground invasion materialized. Signals a new phase of Axis of Resistance integration.

stories.title: Pentagon Pizza Index
stories.detail: Late-night food delivery to Pentagon/Langley correlates with crisis activity.

stories.title: Social Media Surge
stories.detail: #IranConflict #OperationEpicFury #HormuzCrisis trending. Track for crowd signals and breaking developments.

stories.title: US Base Damage
stories.detail: Hit 11 of ~20 US bases in region. 17+ sites confirmed damaged by satellite. $800M+ in infrastructure damage (BBC). 6 US personnel killed at Camp Arifjan alone.

stories.title: US Vetoed Ceasefire Resolution
stories.detail: US vetoed Russia's UNSC resolution calling for an end to all hostilities (March 11). Only passed UNSC Resolution 2817 condemning Iran (13-0, China/Russia abstaining). US veto of ceasefire gave Iran the narrative that the rules-based order was structurally rigged against it.

stories.title: War Started During Negotiations
stories.detail: Oman FM declared diplomatic "breakthrough" on Feb 27. Iran's FM Araghchi said deal "within reach" on Feb 25. US-Israel struck anyway on Feb 28. This diplomatic context — war launched during active negotiations — drove 15+ countries to condemn the strikes and gave Iran the global narrative.

stories.title: Iran Air Campaign
stories.detail:
13,000+ strikes over 38 days of major combat (CENTCOM Admiral Cooper, Apr 9). Iranian air force grounded; 150+ naval vessels destroyed; 190+ ballistic missile launchers destroyed; ~80% of nuclear industrial base struck. Ceasefire halted strikes as of Apr 7. Threatened to resume if ceasefire collapses.

CENTCOM: 90% Iran weapons factories hit, 80% missile facilities, 80% nuclear industrial base. VP Vance: energy infrastructure strikes — next threshold if talks fail. Politico: Pentagon officials warned US was "running out of strategically important targets."

meta.keyFact: CENTCOM: 90% Iran weapons factories hit, 80% missile facilities, 80% nuclear industrial base. VP Vance: energy infrastructure strikes — next threshold if talks fail. Politico: Pentagon officials warned US was "running out of strategically important targets."

stories.title: Iraqi Militias
stories.detail:
Pro-Iranian groups in Iraq announced parallel 2-week suspension of attacks on "enemy bases" following ceasefire. Previously: 19–41 drone/rocket attacks/day by Islamic Resistance in Iraq; BP North Rumaila oilfield hit (Apr 4); Chinese-operated Maysan oilfield hit (Apr 5). Ceasefire compliance uncertain given Hezbollah resumed after Lebanon strikes.

Apr 5: Chinese-operated Maysan oilfield struck — first targeting of Chinese-operated infrastructure. US counter-strikes hit 53rd PMF Brigade HQ (Tal Afar, Apr 1) and 45th PMF Brigade HQ (Anbar, Apr 4). Militia pause may dissolve if Lebanon ceasefire collapses.

meta.keyFact: Apr 5: Chinese-operated Maysan oilfield struck — first targeting of Chinese-operated infrastructure. US counter-strikes hit 53rd PMF Brigade HQ (Tal Afar, Apr 1) and 45th PMF Brigade HQ (Anbar, Apr 4). Militia pause may dissolve if Lebanon ceasefire collapses.

stories.title: Satellite Imagery
stories.detail: Copernicus Sentinel-2 + Bellingcat BDA. ISW publishes verified satellite damage assessments.

stories.title: Seismic Activity (Iran)
stories.detail: USGS data for Iran bounding box. Large munitions/bunker-busters register as seismic events.

stories.title: 2,400+ Interceptors Expended
stories.detail: As of March 30, coalition forces expended at least 2,400 interceptors — approaching known prewar stockpiles (Bloomberg). Iran's Shahed drones ($20–50k each) systematically exhausted high-cost US/allied interceptors ($1M–$3M each). Forced US to redeploy THAAD from South Korea, creating Pacific deterrence gaps.

stories.title: BGP Routing Anomalies
stories.detail: Iran's internet backbone routing near-completely disrupted since Day 1. IODA shows near-zero traffic.

stories.title: BeiDou Navigation System (China)
stories.detail: China provided Iran access to BeiDou satellite navigation — encrypted military signals less vulnerable to Western jamming, sub-meter accuracy, mid-flight redirection up to 2,000 km. Confirmed by French intel chief Alain Juillet and CIA Director Ratcliffe. Explains marked improvement in Iranian missile accuracy vs. June 2025 conflict.
stories.sourceText: Al Jazeera March 11 / Small Wars Journal / CNBC March 26

stories.title: Ceasefire Extracted After 39 Days — CRITICAL ACHIEVEMENT
stories.detail: Iran survived 39 days of sustained bombardment (13,000+ strikes, Iranian air force grounded, 150+ naval vessels destroyed) and extracted a ceasefire without capitulating on its core demands. Iran's SNSC declared the ceasefire "an enduring defeat" for Washington. Both Pakistan (primary broker) and China (last-minute pressure) intervened to preserve Iran's position. Iran entered Islamabad talks with Hormuz leverage intact and Lebanon exclusion as its primary bargaining chip. Independent analysts (Ritter, Escobar, Galbraith/The Nation): ceasefire validates Iran's strategy of strategic patience and asymmetric denial.
stories.sourceText: CENTCOM / Wikipedia / Al Jazeera / The Nation / Scott Ritter YouTube Apr 9

stories.title: China: BeiDou + Chemical Precursors
stories.detail: China provided BeiDou navigation access (dramatically improving accuracy) and — per CNA analysis — two sanctioned Iranian vessels picked up chemicals at Zhuhai port including sodium perchlorate (rocket propellant). Chinese electronics markets continued supplying inertial sensors and navigation modules for missile guidance.
stories.sourceText: CNA March 24 / Atlantic Council March 25 / Al Jazeera

events.detail: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

events.detail: The Tanker War between Iran and Iraq in the 1980s also disrupted global shipping. But the situation today is different.

stories.title: Hormuz Toll Booth
stories.detail: Began charging $2M per voyage for IRGC-approved passage (Bloomberg, March 24). 26 ships transited under IRGC escort; vessels from China, Russia, Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Iraq approved. US/Israeli-linked ships denied. Iran parliament moved to formalize toll legislation.

stories.title: Lebanon / Hezbollah
stories.summary: ESCALATING DESPITE CEASEFIRE — OPERATION ETERNAL DARKNESS
stories.detail:
Operation Eternal Darkness (Apr 8): Israel's largest single strike wave on Lebanon — ~50 IAF jets, ~160 munitions, 100+ Hezbollah command centers/military installations. Strikes hit Beirut southern suburbs, central areas, Beqaa Valley, southern Lebanon. 254 killed, 1,000+ injured. Five IDF divisions in Lebanon (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd). 98th Division near full control of Bint Jbail (Apr 9). 7 key bridges destroyed; southern Lebanon cut off. Hezbollah resumed rocket attacks on northern Israel (dozens fired Apr 9).

Netanyahu: "Lebanon is NOT in the ceasefire." Vance: "We never made such a commitment." Arab League: Israel "persistently seeking to sabotage" Iran ceasefire. IDF announced area south of Litani River "disconnected from Lebanon." Lebanon declared day of mourning. 1,784+ killed total in Lebanon; 1M+ displaced.

meta.keyFact: Netanyahu: "Lebanon is NOT in the ceasefire." Vance: "We never made such a commitment." Arab League: Israel "persistently seeking to sabotage" Iran ceasefire. IDF announced area south of Litani River "disconnected from Lebanon." Lebanon declared day of mourning. 1,784+ killed total in Lebanon; 1M+ displaced.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz
stories.detail:
Ceasefire Day 1 (Apr 8): Only 4 vessels passed with AIS active (Kpler data). Iran re-blocked after Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness. By Apr 9: oil tankers halted again per Iran's Fars News. IRGC published mine chart routing ships northward near Larak Island — implies mines in traditional Oman-side corridor. Cap: ≤15 vessels/day with IRGC approval; fees: ~$1/barrel (Iran's share for reconstruction). 400+ tankers stuck in Gulf. UK FM: Hormuz "must be toll-free."

Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

meta.keyFact: Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

stories.title: Cluster Munition Escalation
stories.detail: By late March, ~70% of Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Israel carry cluster warheads (up from 50% on March 10 per IDF). Cluster munition killed 2 civilians in Ramat Gan apartment (March 17); killed 2 workers in Yehud (March 9). Strategy overwhelms Iron Dome via area denial.

stories.title: Cyber Operations — 60+ Groups
stories.detail: 60+ hacktivist groups active by March 2; Electronic Operations Room coordinating Cyber Islamic Resistance, KillNet, Ghosts of Palestine, RipperSec, 313 Team (Iraq), and others. Claimed SCADA access to Israeli/Gulf industrial systems. DDoS and data-wiping operations on financial, defense, and energy infrastructure. Collaborated with pro-Russian groups.
stories.sourceText: Palo Alto Unit42 / Axios March 17 / Resecurity March 24

stories.title: Ceasefire Extracted After 39 Days — CRITICAL ACHIEVEMENT
stories.detail: Iran survived 39 days of sustained bombardment (13,000+ strikes, Iranian air force grounded, 150+ naval vessels destroyed) and extracted a ceasefire without capitulating on its core demands. Iran's SNSC declared the ceasefire "an enduring defeat" for Washington. Both Pakistan (primary broker) and China (last-minute pressure) intervened to preserve Iran's position. Iran entered Islamabad talks with Hormuz leverage intact and Lebanon exclusion as its primary bargaining chip. Independent analysts (Ritter, Escobar, Galbraith/The Nation): ceasefire validates Iran's strategy of strategic patience and asymmetric denial.
stories.sourceText: CENTCOM / Wikipedia / Al Jazeera / The Nation / Scott Ritter YouTube Apr 9

stories.title: Hormuz Toll Booth
stories.detail: Began charging $2M per voyage for IRGC-approved passage (Bloomberg, March 24). 26 ships transited under IRGC escort; vessels from China, Russia, Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Iraq approved. US/Israeli-linked ships denied. Iran parliament moved to formalize toll legislation.

stories.title: EU Refused Hormuz Role
stories.detail: EU leaders explicitly rejected military involvement in Strait of Hormuz (March 16). NATO Article 5 not invoked. Only direct US allies participated. 15+ countries condemned US-Israel strikes including China, Russia, Turkey, South Africa, Brazil, Pakistan, Indonesia, Malaysia, Iraq, Syria, and multiple African nations.

events.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
events.detail: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

events.title: Iran attacks ships in Hormuz Strait as the U.S. continues its blockade amid ceasefire
events.detail: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade. The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension. Iran's top negotiator says it's "not possible to reopen" the strait due to "blatent violations" of US-Iran ceasefire. President Trump says he ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill any boat" putting mines in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. ramps up minesweeping there. President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.

events.title: Russia could be ready for NATO conflict year after Ukraine, Dutch warn
events.detail: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat the alliance militarily, but to divide it through limited territorial gains, a new intelligence report states.

events.title: Iran Rejects US 45-Day Ceasefire Proposal as "Illogical"
events.detail: Iranian FM spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei publicly called the US-brokered 45-day ceasefire proposal "illogical" and "unacceptable," rejecting the framework delivered via Pakistan. Iran's stated reasons: it cannot negotiate under military threat while US strikes continue; temporary ceasefires benefit the US by giving time to rearm; the 15-point US plan was "exceedingly ambitious." Iran submitted its own 10-point counter-proposal including full war reparations, permanent cessation of all regional conflicts, full US military withdrawal from Gulf states, and Iran's right to charge Hormuz transit fees.

events.title: Kuwait International Airport Fuel Tanks Hit by Iranian Drone
events.detail: An Iranian drone struck fuel storage tanks at Kuwait International Airport, causing significant fire and damage. No casualties were reported. The strike demonstrated Iran's continued ability to hit civilian infrastructure in Gulf states despite sustained degradation of its drone inventory. Kuwait scrambled F/A-18s in response.

events.title: Reuters: Turkey Serving as Back-Channel Messenger Between Iran and US
events.detail: Reuters confirmed that Turkey was conveying messages between Iran and the United States, acting as the primary back-channel in the absence of direct diplomatic communication. Turkey's FM Hakan Fidan had declared Israel the "primary responsible party" for the war while simultaneously facilating de-escalation messaging. Turkey blocked US proposals to use Kurdish forces against Iran and was serving as the key diplomatic node for any eventual negotiated settlement.

events.title: GCC Food Emergency: 70% of Imports Disrupted, Prices Up 40–120%
events.detail: Gulf Cooperation Council states — which rely on Hormuz for over 80% of their caloric intake — declared a growing food supply emergency by mid-March. 70% of food imports were disrupted. Consumer food prices across the Gulf rose 40–120%. Retailers including Lulu Retail began emergency airlift-shipping of staple foods. India shut down 3 of its urea plants due to gas shortages; Bangladesh closed 4 of its 5 fertilizer plants.

events.title: UNSC Resolution 2817: Condemns Iran — US Vetoes Russian Ceasefire Resolution
events.detail: Bahrain (on behalf of 6 Gulf states + Jordan) introduced UNSC Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's retaliatory strikes — but notably NOT the original US-Israel strikes. Vote: 13-0 in favor with China and Russia abstaining. Russia simultaneously proposed a separate resolution calling for an end to the entire war; the United States vetoed it. Iran framed the veto as proof that the "rules-based order" was structurally rigged against non-Western states.

stories.title: Hormuz De Facto Closure
stories.detail: Closed Strait of Hormuz — 20% of global oil, 20–25% of global LNG, 35% of global urea/fertilizer. ~3,000 vessels waiting as of late March (S&P Global). Normal daily traffic of 120 ships reduced to near standstill. First closure of Hormuz in modern history.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz
stories.detail:
Ceasefire Day 1 (Apr 8): Only 4 vessels passed with AIS active (Kpler data). Iran re-blocked after Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness. By Apr 9: oil tankers halted again per Iran's Fars News. IRGC published mine chart routing ships northward near Larak Island — implies mines in traditional Oman-side corridor. Cap: ≤15 vessels/day with IRGC approval; fees: ~$1/barrel (Iran's share for reconstruction). 400+ tankers stuck in Gulf. UK FM: Hormuz "must be toll-free."

Iran and Oman co-managing maritime protocol. TASS: Iran restricting passage to max 15 vessels/day. Local 10/WPLG confirmed chart shows ships routed away from "danger zones." VP Vance: "signs straits are starting to reopen" — contradicted by Iranian state media.

In the past week, the U.S. interdicted three ships – two in the Indian Ocean and one in the Strait of Hormuz – while Iran went after three ships, seizing two. There are reports that the third ship was able to continue its journey. events.title: 10 Senior IRGC Commanders Killed — Days 32-39 Targeted Killing Tally
events.detail: Between March 31 and April 7, US-Israeli targeted killings eliminated at least 10 senior IRGC and Artesh commanders: Eshaghi (AFGS adviser), Mohammad Sadeghi (IRGC missile tunnel engineer), Mehdi Vafaei (Quds Force Lebanon underground chief), Makram Atimi (ballistic missile chief), Gen. Mohammad Ali Fathali Zadeh (Fatehin Battalions), Yusuf Ismail Hashem (Hezbollah Southern Front commander), and multiple battalion-level officers. Adding to the 28 named senior figures eliminated in Days 1-31, Iran had lost approximately 38+ general and senior officers killed in six weeks of war — an unprecedented command attrition rate.
events.sourceText: ISW / Critical Threats / Wikipedia (2026 Iran War) (Days 32-39)

events.title: Houthis Claim Joint Cruise Missile and UAV Attack on Israel With Iran and Hezbollah
events.detail: IDF reported several UAVs from Yemen aimed at Eilat in southern Israel. Houthis claimed a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah combining cruise missiles and UAVs. All were intercepted. The coordinated three-front announcement — Houthis, Hezbollah, and Iran simultaneously — was intended to demonstrate the persistence of the "Axis of Resistance's" operational coordination despite six weeks of sustained coalition strikes.
events.sourceText: IDF / Wikipedia (2026 Houthi Strikes on Israel) (April 6, 2026)

events.title: Hezbollah Anti-Ship Cruise Missile Strikes Israeli Warship
events.detail: Hezbollah fired an anti-ship cruise missile at an Israeli Navy warship operating in Lebanese coastal waters — the first confirmed Hezbollah anti-ship cruise missile strike of the conflict. The missile hit and caused confirmed damage to the vessel. The attack demonstrated Hezbollah's retention of advanced anti-ship capabilities despite Israel's claim to have significantly degraded Hezbollah. Hezbollah's Telegram channel posted footage of the launch.

events.title: Houthis Conduct 8th Attack on Israel — Coordination With Iran and Hezbollah
events.detail: Houthis launched their 8th attack on Israel since March 28, targeting Ben Gurion Airport and military sites in southern Israel with a cluster missile and several drones. All were intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Houthis claimed coordination with Iran and Hezbollah in a joint operation. The Houthis had not yet resumed commercial shipping attacks in the Red Sea as of April 7, but explicitly threatened to impose a naval blockade at Bab al-Mandab targeting "aggressor country" vessels.
